locations.name: Thornwall Village
locations.description: A humble frontier village with a tavern, blacksmith, and general store. Adventurers gather here before heading into the wilds.

locations.name: Whispering Woods
locations.description: Ancient trees creak with forgotten voices. Strange lights drift between the trunks. Wolves and worse lurk in the undergrowth.

locations.name: King's Road
locations.description: The crumbling highway that once connected great cities. Now bandits and beasts prowl its overgrown stones.

locations.name: Ruined Shrine
locations.description: Cracked marble columns draped in moss. Something unholy stirs in the crypt below.

locations.name: Dark Hollow
locations.description: A cave system carved by ancient rivers. Goblins have fortified the entrance with crude palisades.

locations.name: Ironhold Fortress
locations.description: A dwarven trade outpost built into the mountainside. Master smiths and rare goods.

locations.name: The Crossroads
locations.description: Where four paths meet under a hanging tree. Merchants, mercenaries, and monsters all pass through.

locations.name: Ember Mines
locations.description: Abandoned dwarven mines now infested with fire elementals and worse. Rich veins of precious ore still glint in the walls.

locations.name: Shadowfen Marsh
locations.description: Toxic mist clings to the bog. Undead shamble through the murk and will-o-wisps lure the unwary.

locations.name: Sunspire Citadel
locations.description: A gleaming city of high towers and learned scholars. The Mage Academy and Grand Cathedral stand at its heart.

locations.name: Witch's Tower
locations.description: A crooked spire rising from the swamp. Dark sorcery crackles around its peak.

locations.name: Dragon Peak
locations.description: The volcanic summit where the ancient wyrm Ignavoth makes its lair. Only the bravest dare climb.

apprentice-wand.name: Apprentice Wand
apprentice-wand.description: A smooth yew wand etched with beginner runes.

runed-oak-staff.name: Runed Oak Staff
runed-oak-staff.description: A travel staff carved with patient woodland sigils.

ember-wand.name: Ember Wand
ember-wand.description: Its tip glows with a coal-red spark that never dies.

battlemage-rod.name: Battlemage Rod
battlemage-rod.description: A heavy focus built for spellcasters who stand their ground.

starfire-staff.name: Starfire Staff
starfire-staff.description: A polished ash staff crowned with a shard of fallen star.

witch-staff.name: Staff of the Fen Witch
witch-staff.description: Twisted wood pulsing with dark power.

cinder-circlet.name: Cinder Circlet
cinder-circlet.description: A scorched crown that sharpens the mind beneath falling ash.

dragon-heart.name: Dragon's Heart
dragon-heart.description: The still-warm heart of an ancient wyrm. Radiates immense power.

dragon-soul-orb.name: Dragon Soul Orb
dragon-soul-orb.description: A crystal sphere humming with the memories of draconic flame.

novice-robes.name: Novice Robes
novice-robes.description: Simple robes lined with thread meant to hold cantrips.

mystic-robe.name: Mystic Robe
mystic-robe.description: Layered blue cloth stitched with warding script.

archmage-raiment.name: Archmage Raiment
archmage-raiment.description: A regal mantle embroidered with constellations and circles of power.

enchanted-plate.name: Enchanted Plate
enchanted-plate.description: Armor animated by sorcery, now stilled.

voidweave-robes.name: Voidweave Robes
voidweave-robes.description: Black silk woven through with threads that drink moonlight.

cantrip-tome.name: Cantrip Tome
cantrip-tome.description: A slim spellbook carried in the off-hand by hedge mages.

rune-grimoire.name: Rune Grimoire
rune-grimoire.description: A chained grimoire whose iron clasps hum with warding power.

drake-scale.name: Drake Scale Shield
drake-scale.description: Scale of a young drake, fashioned into a shield.

drakescript-codex.name: Drakescript Codex
drakescript-codex.description: A lacquered codex inked with fireproof draconic formulas.

silver-focus-ring.name: Silver Focus Ring
silver-focus-ring.description: A narrow ring that steadies spellcasting gestures.

hydra-eye-charm.name: Hydra Eye Charm
hydra-eye-charm.description: A murky talisman said to let a sorcerer think in many directions at once.

wraith-essence.name: Wraith Essence
wraith-essence.description: A cold, swirling mist in a vial.

golem-heart.name: Golem Heart
golem-heart.description: A heavy stone etched with runes.

rat-tail.name: Rat Tail
rat-tail.description: Disgusting but someone will buy it.

ashwood-bow.name: Ashwood Bow
ashwood-bow.description: A light hunting bow cut from frontier ashwood.

trail-leathers.name: Trail Leathers
trail-leathers.description: Supple leathers dyed to disappear beneath leaves and dusk.

fletcher-quiver.name: Fletcher Quiver
fletcher-quiver.description: An offhand quiver balanced for quick draws and steady footing.

hunter-charm.name: Hunter's Charm
hunter-charm.description: A carved tooth charm worn by scouts who trust their instincts.

yew-longbow.name: Yew Longbow
yew-longbow.description: A strong longbow that rewards patience, breath, and distance.

scout-mantle.name: Scout Mantle
scout-mantle.description: A weatherproof cloak and leather harness favored by border wardens.

barbed-quiver.name: Barbed Quiver
barbed-quiver.description: An offhand arrow-rig packed with hooked shafts for brutal volleys.

falcon-talon-charm.name: Falcon Talon Charm
falcon-talon-charm.description: A silver-wrapped talon said to sharpen a ranger's eye at dawn.

moonshot-bow.name: Moonshot Bow
moonshot-bow.description: A pale bowstring sings softly whenever moonlight touches its limbs.

warden-cloak.name: Warden Cloak
warden-cloak.description: A layered cloak of treated hide and feather-light shadowcloth.

stormfeather-quiver.name: Stormfeather Quiver
stormfeather-quiver.description: A griffon-feather quiver that turns motion into relentless pressure.

hawksight-talisman.name: Hawksight Talisman
hawksight-talisman.description: A polished lens and bone charm that narrows the world to one perfect shot.

militia-blade.name: Militia Blade
militia-blade.description: A serviceable sword carried by village levies and stubborn survivors.

tower-heater.name: Tower Heater
tower-heater.description: A broad iron-rimmed shield built to hold a line under arrow fire.

vanguard-mail.name: Vanguard Mail
vanguard-mail.description: Linked mail reinforced at the shoulders for relentless front-line pushes.

knight-bastard-sword.name: Knight Bastard Sword
knight-bastard-sword.description: A two-handed noble blade equally suited to duels and battlefield chaos.

lioncrest-plate.name: Lioncrest Plate
lioncrest-plate.description: Golden-etched plate once worn by the sworn defenders of a fallen keep.

war-banner-charm.name: War Banner Charm
war-banner-charm.description: A braided cord cut from an old battle standard. It stirs courage in the blood.

colossus-greatblade.name: Colossus Greatblade
colossus-greatblade.description: A massive blade forged to break monsters rather than men.

bulwark-aegis.name: Bulwark Aegis
bulwark-aegis.description: A fortress of a shield whose weight feels lighter in a hero's grasp.

thief-stiletto.name: Thief Stiletto
thief-stiletto.description: A narrow dagger made for ribs, locks, and sudden exits.

dusk-leathers.name: Dusk Leathers
dusk-leathers.description: Supple dark leathers that move without a whisper.

smoke-pouch.name: Smoke Pouch
smoke-pouch.description: An offhand satchel of soot bombs and sleight-of-hand tricks.

lucky-bone.name: Lucky Bone
lucky-bone.description: A tiny carved charm every cutpurse swears kept them alive one more night.

shadow-daggers.name: Shadow Daggers
shadow-daggers.description: Matched blades meant to flash once and be remembered too late.

nightcloak.name: Nightcloak
nightcloak.description: A charcoal cloak that melts into alleys, reeds, and midnight hedgerows.

poison-kit-satchel.name: Poison Kit Satchel
poison-kit-satchel.description: An offhand satchel of venoms, wire, and razor tools.

cutpurse-signet.name: Cutpurse Signet
cutpurse-signet.description: A stolen noble seal repurposed as proof that bold lies often work.

whisperknife.name: Whisperknife
whisperknife.description: A near-weightless blade that parts cloth and flesh with equal ease.

gloomweave.name: Gloomweave Jerkin
gloomweave.description: A sleek jerkin woven with greythread that confuses the eye at a glance.

assassin-kit.name: Assassin Kit
assassin-kit.description: A lacquered wrist-rig of vials, picks, and throwing needles.

raven-mark.name: Raven Mark
raven-mark.description: An obsidian brooch traded only among killers with reputations to protect.

pilgrim-mace.name: Pilgrim Mace
pilgrim-mace.description: A humble iron mace blessed for the road and whatever walks it after dark.

acolyte-vestments.name: Acolyte Vestments
acolyte-vestments.description: Plain but reverent vestments sewn with protective scripture.

scripture.name: Pocket Scripture
scripture.description: An offhand prayerbook carried by ministers, healers, and exorcists.

sun-charm.name: Sun Charm
sun-charm.description: A brass sunburst worn close to the heart during long vigils.

sanctified-mace.name: Sanctified Mace
sanctified-mace.description: A silvered mace that rings like a bell when it strikes undead bone.

templar-vestments.name: Templar Vestments
templar-vestments.description: Layered clerical armor for those who bring mercy with a shield-arm.

reliquary.name: Reliquary
reliquary.description: A bronze reliquary box carried offhand, warm with old blessings.

prayer-beads.name: Prayer Beads
prayer-beads.description: Each bead marks a vow, a loss, or a miracle survived.

dawnbringer-crozier.name: Dawnbringer Crozier
dawnbringer-crozier.description: A radiant staff-mace whose golden head burns brightest before sunrise.

cathedral-robes.name: Cathedral Robes
cathedral-robes.description: High ceremonial robes layered with hidden wards and ash-proof lining.

gospel-codex.name: Gospel Codex
gospel-codex.description: A weighty offhand tome whose illuminated pages flare against corruption.

halo-seal.name: Halo Seal
halo-seal.description: A thin circle of blessed silver that settles over its bearer like calm.

example-moon-blade.name: Example Moon Blade
example-moon-blade.description: Example content item for API-driven expansion.

ironwall-greathelm.name: Ironwall Greathelm
ironwall-greathelm.description: A reinforced plate helm for soldiers who advance into fire without hesitation.

runic-cowl.name: Runic Cowl
runic-cowl.description: A hood stitched with containment runes that quiet unstable magic near the skull.

viper-mask.name: Viper Mask
viper-mask.description: A sleek venomous-green mask that dulls the wearer's presence and sharpens lethal intent.

templar-crest-helm.name: Templar Crest Helm
templar-crest-helm.description: A cresthelm blessed at the cathedral's high anvil, worn by ordained holy warriors.

windtracker-cap.name: Windtracker Cap
windtracker-cap.description: A waxed hunting cap fitted with a hidden compass needle for tracking prey into the wind.

warlord-warhelm.name: Warlord's Warhelm
warlord-warhelm.description: A fearsome warhelm passed between warlords whose names echo through history as legend.

lich-crown.name: Lich Crown
lich-crown.description: A crown of frozen bone worn by a sorcerer who outlived every empire they served.

phantom-veil.name: Phantom Veil
phantom-veil.description: A veil that makes the wearer a ghost between heartbeats; witnesses see only shadow.

seraph-halo.name: Seraph Halo
seraph-halo.description: An ethereal halo granted by a dying angel to a champion of absolute and unshaken faith.

hurricane-hood.name: Hurricane Hood
hurricane-hood.description: Woven from stormclouds by a ranger who survived seven hurricanes alone in the wilds.

iron-vice-gauntlets.name: Iron Vice Gauntlets
iron-vice-gauntlets.description: Heavy gauntlets that turn every grip into a crushing vice against blade and bone.

runehand-gloves.name: Runehand Gloves
runehand-gloves.description: Silk gloves inscribed with power-amplifying sigils that sharpen every spell cast.

knifework-wraps.name: Knifework Wraps
knifework-wraps.description: Tight wraps that balance the fingers over blade edges and let daggers fly fast and true.

consecrated-grips.name: Consecrated Grips
consecrated-grips.description: Gloves blessed on a cathedral high altar; steady for mace strokes and healing rites alike.

bowmaster-grips.name: Bowmaster Grips
bowmaster-grips.description: Waxed leather grips optimized for rapid-fire draw; the bowstring never bites the skin.

crushers-of-doom.name: Crushers of Doom
crushers-of-doom.description: Gauntlets that make every blow a lethal mauling. Blood coats the iron knuckles constantly.

arcanist-foci-gloves.name: Arcanist Foci Gloves
arcanist-foci-gloves.description: Gloves that channel raw magic through crystal fingertip foci with surgical precision.

nightfall-wraps.name: Nightfall Wraps
nightfall-wraps.description: Dark wraps that drink enemy blood to power the next strike. They never stay clean.

saintly-grasp.name: Saintly Grasp
saintly-grasp.description: Holy hands that bind wounds and shackle foes alike; touched by the grace of two saints.

predator-grips.name: Predator Grips
predator-grips.description: Grips soaked in marsh extract that track and slow prey through scent and instinct alone.

titan-gauntlets.name: Titan Gauntlets
titan-gauntlets.description: Gauntlets forged for a titan. The earth itself buckles beneath each crushing strike.

void-hands.name: Void Hands
void-hands.description: Hands shaped from void matter that incinerate whatever they touch with cold black flame.

shadowstrike-wraps.name: Shadowstrike Wraps
shadowstrike-wraps.description: Wraps made from shadow silk; each strike is an execution, each scratch a slow death.

hallowed-hands.name: Hallowed Hands
hallowed-hands.description: Gloves blessed by three saints in sequence; healing and righteous smiting in equal measure.

galeforce-grips.name: Galeforce Grips
galeforce-grips.description: Grips that channel wind itself, making every arrow fly with the force of a full storm.

ironmarch-boots.name: Ironmarch Boots
ironmarch-boots.description: Armored boots built for punching through enemy lines without losing a single stride.

glyphstep-sandals.name: Glyphstep Sandals
glyphstep-sandals.description: Sandals traced with leylines that sharpen spell focus with each measured step forward.

shadowstep-boots.name: Shadowstep Boots
shadowstep-boots.description: Boots engineered for complete silence on stone, wood, and compacted earth alike.

pilgrim-greaves.name: Pilgrim's Greaves
pilgrim-greaves.description: Road-greaves worn thin by holy pilgrimage; sanctified enough to still turn blades.

swifttrack-boots.name: Swifttrack Boots
swifttrack-boots.description: Lightweight hunting boots for closing distance without making a sound or a stumble.

warbreaker-stompers.name: Warbreaker Stompers
warbreaker-stompers.description: Boots that turn a charge into a ground-shaking impact that leaves enemies bleeding.

spellthread-sandals.name: Spellthread Sandals
spellthread-sandals.description: Sandals threaded with living spellwork that sustains the caster through a long battle.

wraithstep-boots.name: Wraithstep Boots
wraithstep-boots.description: Boots of a wraith-dancer who leaves no footprints behind, only bleeding enemies.

sacred-treads.name: Sacred Treads
sacred-treads.description: Blessed treads that sanctify every step, protecting the faithful from those who pursue them.

windstrider-boots.name: Windstrider Boots
windstrider-boots.description: Boots that move with the wind and leave pursuing enemies gasping in slowed misery.

godstomper-boots.name: Godstomper Boots
godstomper-boots.description: Boots forged in a godwar, still carrying the crushing force of that final charge.

leyline-walkers.name: Leyline Walkers
leyline-walkers.description: Sandals that walk the leylines, drawing raw power from every step into open ground.

midnight-tread.name: Midnight Tread
midnight-tread.description: Born from shadow and malice; the wearer becomes the dark itself, utterly unstoppable.

heavenstrider-greaves.name: Heavenstrider Greaves
heavenstrider-greaves.description: Greaves touched by divine light; they carry the bearer forward through any storm.

stormstrider-boots.name: Stormstrider Boots
stormstrider-boots.description: Woven through with storm energy; the wearer covers ground at the speed of lightning.

iron-gorget.name: Iron Gorget
iron-gorget.description: A crude iron gorget that protects the throat and steels the will for front-line combat.

focus-pendant.name: Focus Pendant
focus-pendant.description: A crystal pendant that steadies the mind and sharpens spell focus under battle pressure.

shadow-cord.name: Shadow Cord
shadow-cord.description: A knotted cord worn against skin that dampens footfall and quickens movement.

pilgrim-medallion.name: Pilgrim Medallion
pilgrim-medallion.description: A worn holy medallion carried by those who walk sacred roads seeking divine guidance.

hunter-locket.name: Hunter's Locket
hunter-locket.description: A small locket holding a tuft of prey fur and a carved arrow for tracking luck.

warlord-pendant.name: Warlord's Pendant
warlord-pendant.description: A pendant stamped with the iron seal of a warlord; grants fierce resolve in the line of battle.

arcane-talisman.name: Arcane Talisman
arcane-talisman.description: A talisman crackling with concentrated spell energy between polished obsidian plates.

blade-locket.name: Blade Locket
blade-locket.description: A locket fitted with a hidden blade and battle-honed runes; luck and killing edge in one.

holy-pendant.name: Holy Pendant
holy-pendant.description: A pure silver pendant consecrated at the Grand Temple, worn by devoted holy warriors.

eagle-torc.name: Eagle Torc
eagle-torc.description: A torc shaped as a diving eagle that sharpens the eyes and steadies the marksman's aim.

berserker-torc.name: Berserker's Torc
berserker-torc.description: A blood-iron torc worn by berserkers who made blood pacts with fury itself.

void-pendant.name: Void Pendant
void-pendant.description: A pendant holding a sliver of void that slowly drains the strength from nearby enemies.

shadow-fang-necklace.name: Shadow Fang Necklace
shadow-fang-necklace.description: A necklace of viper fangs; each hit draws blood back to the wearer's veins.

radiant-reliquary.name: Radiant Reliquary
radiant-reliquary.description: A reliquary holding a saint's fingerbone that radiates soft healing light in battle.

storm-torc.name: Storm Torc
storm-torc.description: A thunder-charged torc that slows enemies caught within the wearer's storm path.

dragonbone-gorget.name: Dragonbone Gorget
dragonbone-gorget.description: A gorget cut from a dragon's neck bone; grants draconic savagery to any warrior.

star-soul-pendant.name: Star Soul Pendant
star-soul-pendant.description: A pendant containing a captured star-soul; rains celestial fire upon those it opposes.

abyssal-medallion.name: Abyssal Medallion
abyssal-medallion.description: A medallion from the deep abyss; each wound feeds the wearer's insatiable hunger.

divine-crown-pendant.name: Divine Crown Pendant
divine-crown-pendant.description: A pendant shaped as a divine crown; grants near-angelic resilience to any true believer.

tempest-amulet.name: Tempest Amulet
tempest-amulet.description: An amulet filled with imprisoned tempest; the air itself becomes a weapon in the wearer's hands.

warchiefs-cleaver.name: Warchief's Cleaver
warchiefs-cleaver.description: A brutal cleaver wrenched from the grip of a goblin warlord. The blade still hums with bloodlust.

soulbound-vestments.name: Soulbound Vestments
soulbound-vestments.description: Robes woven from the prayers of a hundred clerics, bound to the soul of whoever dons them.

molten-core-gauntlets.name: Molten Core Gauntlets
molten-core-gauntlets.description: Gauntlets forged around a living ember. The heat never fades and neither does the grip.

hexweave-mantle.name: Hexweave Mantle
hexweave-mantle.description: A cloak of living spellwork stripped from the Fen Witch. It whispers cantrips in dead languages.

ignavoths-fang.name: Ignavoth's Fang
ignavoths-fang.description: A tooth the size of a greatsword, torn from the maw of the ancient dragon Ignavoth. Radiates primal fury.

copper-band.name: Copper Band
copper-band.description: A tarnished copper ring stamped with a fist.

willow-ring.name: Willow Ring
willow-ring.description: A thin ring carved from sacred willow wood.

signet-of-valor.name: Signet of Valor
signet-of-valor.description: A signet ring awarded to soldiers who held the line.

spellthread-band.name: Spellthread Band
spellthread-band.description: Woven from enchanted thread that hums when spells are near.

quicksilver-ring.name: Quicksilver Ring
quicksilver-ring.description: A liquid-silver band that seems to shift and flow.

devotion-ring.name: Devotion Ring
devotion-ring.description: Blessed by a traveling priest during the autumn rites.

ranger-loop.name: Ranger's Loop
ranger-loop.description: A braided leather ring worn by frontier scouts.

warhammer-seal.name: Warhammer Seal
warhammer-seal.description: An iron seal ring bearing the mark of a siege-breaker company.

leystone-band.name: Leystone Band
leystone-band.description: Cut from stone found at a ley-line convergence.

pathfinder-ring.name: Pathfinder's Ring
pathfinder-ring.description: Worn by elite trailblazers who never lose their way.

conqueror-signet.name: Conqueror's Signet

archmage-loop.name: Archmage's Loop
archmage-loop.description: A ring that bends light around itself, humming with raw arcana.

phantom-ring.name: Phantom Ring

tyrant-seal.name: Tyrant's Seal
tyrant-seal.description: The ruling ring of a conquered kingdom. Its weight feels like authority.

voidloop.name: Voidloop
voidloop.description: A ring of condensed void-matter that whispers forgotten incantations.

shadow-sovereign.name: Shadow Sovereign
shadow-sovereign.description: A pitch-black band that drinks light. Thieves kill for this ring.

saints-circle.name: Saint's Circle
saints-circle.description: A ring worn by the last true saint. It glows faintly in darkness.

horizon-band.name: Horizon Band
horizon-band.description: Forged at the edge of the world where land meets sky.

iron-tooth.name: Iron Tooth
iron-tooth.description: A molar replaced with iron. Warriors wear them as trophies.

focus-shard.name: Focus Shard
focus-shard.description: A sliver of crystal that steadies the mind when gripped.

battle-scar-token.name: Battle Scar Token
battle-scar-token.description: A coin-sized brand mark worn proudly by veterans.

enchanted-compass.name: Enchanted Compass
enchanted-compass.description: Always points toward danger—or away from it, depending on the holder.

runic-dice.name: Runic Dice
runic-dice.description: Loaded with fate magic. Every roll feels deliberate.

blessed-relic.name: Blessed Relic
blessed-relic.description: A fingerbone of a minor saint, wrapped in gold thread.

survival-kit.name: Survival Kit
survival-kit.description: A compact pouch of herbs, wire, and flint. Never leave camp without it.

warlord-tooth.name: Warlord's Tooth
warlord-tooth.description: Ripped from a beast-king. Radiates primal dominance.

void-marble.name: Void Marble
void-marble.description: A marble-sized hole in reality. Stare too long and it stares back.

gambler-coin.name: Gambler's Coin
gambler-coin.description: Heads you win. Tails they lose. It has never landed on its edge.

sacred-bone.name: Sacred Bone
sacred-bone.description: A rib from a temple guardian who died protecting the innocent.

windcatcher-charm.name: Windcatcher Charm
windcatcher-charm.description: A feathered charm that catches favorable winds and ill omens alike.

miracle-splinter.description: A splinter from the True Cross. Wounds close in its presence.

stormcaller-feather.name: Stormcaller Feather
stormcaller-feather.description: A thunderbird feather that crackles with static between your fingers.

leather-cap.name: Leather Cap
leather-cap.description: A simple cap that keeps the rain off and the worst blows softened.

wool-hood.name: Wool Hood

arcanist-circlet.description: A thin silver circlet etched with concentration glyphs.

shadow-cowl.name: Shadow Cowl
shadow-cowl.description: A deep cowl that keeps the face hidden and the mind sharp.

pilgrim-hood.name: Pilgrim's Hood
pilgrim-hood.description: Blessed during the Long Walk. Comforts the faithful.

doomhelm.name: Doomhelm
doomhelm.description: A horned war helm blackened by dragonfire. Inspires terror.

mindfire-crown.name: Mindfire Crown
mindfire-crown.description: A crown of crystallized thought-flame. Spells come faster.

nightwalker-mask.name: Nightwalker Mask
nightwalker-mask.description: A featureless black mask. Those who wear it seem to vanish mid-sentence.

divine-mitre.name: Divine Mitre
divine-mitre.description: A ceremonial mitre that channels divine will into mortal form.

eagle-helm.name: Eagle Helm
eagle-helm.description: A winged helm worn by master rangers who command the frontier.

leather-gloves.name: Leather Gloves
leather-gloves.description: Basic work gloves. Keep the blisters at bay.

wool-mittens.name: Wool Mittens
wool-mittens.description: Warm mittens knit by temple acolytes. Surprisingly comforting.

iron-knuckles.name: Iron Knuckles
iron-knuckles.description: Crude but effective. Good for punching and blocking.

linen-wraps.name: Linen Wraps
linen-wraps.description: Thin wraps that keep fingers nimble for spellcasting.

hide-bracers.name: Hide Bracers
hide-bracers.description: Simple hide bracers worn by hunters and woodsmen.

gauntlets-of-grip.name: Gauntlets of Grip
gauntlets-of-grip.description: Steel gauntlets with etched grip-lines. What you grab stays grabbed.

spellweave-gloves.name: Spellweave Gloves
spellweave-gloves.description: Silk gloves threaded with mana-conductive fibers.

cutpurse-mitts.name: Cutpurse Mitts
cutpurse-mitts.description: Soft leather mitts with padded fingertips. Silence guaranteed.

prayer-wraps.name: Prayer Wraps
prayer-wraps.description: Linen wraps anointed with sacred oils. They smell of frankincense.

bowstring-gloves.name: Bowstring Gloves
bowstring-gloves.description: Reinforced fingertips for rapid nocking and clean releases.

leather-boots.name: Leather Boots
leather-boots.description: Sturdy walking boots. Nothing fancy, everything functional.

cloth-sandals.name: Cloth Sandals
cloth-sandals.description: Light sandals worn by apprentice mages for grounding exercises.

hide-treads.name: Hide Treads
hide-treads.description: Soft-soled treads that barely leave a footprint.

marching-boots.name: Marching Boots
marching-boots.description: Regulation infantry boots. Built to march, not to impress.

pilgrim-sandals.name: Pilgrim Sandals
pilgrim-sandals.description: Simple rope sandals worn on holy pilgrimages.

ironshod-boots.name: Ironshod Boots
ironshod-boots.description: Steel-toed boots that double as weapons in a brawl.

leywalkers.name: Leywalkers
leywalkers.description: Soft boots attuned to ley-line vibrations underfoot.

softpad-boots.name: Softpad Boots
softpad-boots.description: Padded soles for silent movement. A burglar essential.

temple-greaves.name: Temple Greaves
temple-greaves.description: Consecrated greaves worn by temple guardians on night watch.

trailrunner-boots.name: Trailrunner Boots
trailrunner-boots.description: Lightweight boots with reinforced ankles for rough terrain.

worldsplitter.name: Worldsplitter
worldsplitter.description: A greatsword so heavy it cracks the earth with each swing.

staff-of-eternity.name: Staff of Eternity
staff-of-eternity.description: An ancient staff that bends time around its wielder.

eclipse-daggers.name: Eclipse Daggers
eclipse-daggers.description: Twin daggers that cut through shadow and flesh equally.

godtouched-crozier.name: Godtouched Crozier
godtouched-crozier.description: A crozier blessed by a dying god. It hums with fading divinity.

stormbow.name: Stormbow
stormbow.description: A longbow strung with lightning. Arrows arrive before the sound.

aegis-of-the-fallen.name: Aegis of the Fallen
aegis-of-the-fallen.description: A shield bearing the names of every soldier who died holding the line.

grimoire-of-stars.name: Grimoire of Stars
grimoire-of-stars.description: A tome of celestial spells that shields the mind from harm.

quiver-of-whispers.name: Quiver of Whispers
quiver-of-whispers.description: Each arrow drawn from this quiver carries a murmured hex.

shroud-of-knives.name: Shroud of Knives
shroud-of-knives.description: A bandolier of concealed blades that parries as well as any shield.

sacred-codex.name: Sacred Codex
sacred-codex.description: A holy codex that projects divine barriers when opened.

living-bulwark.name: Living Bulwark
living-bulwark.description: A shield of living wood that grows thorns and heals its own cracks.

godplate.name: Godplate
godplate.description: Armor forged by a dead god. Heavier than sin, harder than faith.

leyweave-vestments.name: Leyweave Vestments
leyweave-vestments.description: Robes woven from pure ley-energy. They shimmer between realities.

abyssal-leathers.name: Abyssal Leathers
abyssal-leathers.description: Leather cut from something that should not have had skin.

miracle-vestments.name: Miracle Vestments
miracle-vestments.description: Vestments that glow when prayer is answered. They glow often.

horizon-cloak.name: Horizon Cloak
horizon-cloak.description: A cloak that blends with any terrain. The horizon is always behind you.

tin-locket.name: Tin Locket
tin-locket.description: A cheap locket with a faded portrait inside. Sentimental value only.

glass-bead-necklace.name: Glass Bead Necklace
glass-bead-necklace.description: Colorful glass beads strung on twine. Popular with apprentices.

bone-pendant.name: Bone Pendant
bone-pendant.description: A small animal bone carved into a charm. Keeps the bad luck away, maybe.

wooden-holy-symbol.name: Wooden Holy Symbol
wooden-holy-symbol.description: A hand-carved holy symbol. Simple but sincere.

feather-cord.name: Feather Cord
feather-cord.description: A leather cord strung with hawk feathers. Worn by aspiring rangers.

guild-repair-kit.name: Guild Repair Kit
guild-repair-kit.description: Fully repairs one equipped item. Use anywhere.

quests.title: Wolves at the Gate
quests.description: The village elder asks you to thin the wolf packs threatening livestock in the Whispering Woods.

stages.text: Elder Maren grips your arm with a farmer's hand and a veteran's grip. 'The wolves grow bolder each night — three families lost sheep this week, and last night the Yorrens lost a dog at the pen.' She spreads a rough map and taps the forest edge. 'They den somewhere in the south thicket. Find them.'

choices.label: Study Maren's map for the likely den site
Ask about the wolves' attack patterns
Negotiate for better pay before you go

stages.text: The south thicket grows dark quickly. Claw marks scar every tree below head height, and the undergrowth smells of a large animal that has been sleeping here and eating well. Something watches from the ridge above — patient, unhurried, perfectly still.

choices.label: Move toward the ridge directly
Circle the thicket to find the den entrance
Read the marks and track the pack's movement

stages.text: You find the den: a hollow beneath two fallen oaks, wide enough for a dozen wolves, with scattered bones around the entrance and three sets of tracks going in and two sets coming out. The alpha has left one guard.

choices.label: Draw the guard out with a thrown stone
Wait for the right moment to rush the guard
Watch the guard's rhythm and slip past it

stages.text: The alpha returns at dusk — a large grey wolf with old scars and a long memory. It drops into a hunting crouch the moment it sees you. Behind it, two younger wolves wait at the tree line for a signal.

choices.label: Fight the alpha wolf
Drive it back with fire before it closes
Charge first and break its nerve
Set a snare and bait the alpha in

stages.text: The alpha falls and the younger wolves scatter into the dark without looking back. You collect proof of the kill and begin the walk back to Thornwall in the sudden quiet of a forest that has just lost its apex hunter for the season.

choices.label: Bring back the pelt as proof
Scout the remaining wolves' retreat direction
Return to Maren and report what you found

stages.text: Maren nods with grim satisfaction and presses the agreed pay into your hand. The sheep are safe for now, and the village sleeps with the palisade unguarded for the first time all week.

quests.title: Clear Dark Hollow
quests.description: Goblins have stolen a shipment of iron and fortified a nearby hollow. They need to be driven out.

stages.text: Gruff the blacksmith slams his hammer down hard enough to dent the anvil. 'Those green vermin stole a shipment of iron I spent three months waiting for, and now they've dug themselves into Dark Hollow like they own the place.' He draws a rough sketch of the terrain on his apron and shoves it at you. 'Get my iron back.'

choices.label: Study Gruff's sketch of the hollow carefully
Ask Gruff how many goblins he spotted
Negotiate for better terms before committing
Head straight for Dark Hollow

stages.text: The hollow is ringed with a crude palisade of sharpened sticks, and you can hear goblins arguing on the other side. Two sentries pace the main gap, blades out, clearly bored but alert enough to matter. A second gap sits half-hidden in the undergrowth to the left.

choices.label: Charge the main entrance directly
Slip through the hidden gap on the left
Distract the sentries with a thrown stone
Watch the patrol pattern and time the gap

stages.text: Inside the hollow, a goblin shaman stands on a stump screaming orders at a dozen scurrying grunts who are sorting through the stolen iron with no idea what any of it is for. The shaman spots you immediately and raises its staff.

choices.label: Fight the goblin shaman
Knock the shaman off the stump before it can act
Disrupt the shaman's concentration mid-chant
Use the grunts' confusion to isolate the shaman

stages.text: The shaman is down but a goblin grunt grabs the iron and bolts for the far gap, screaming at the others to follow. Two more turn to cover the retreat with rusty swords. If the iron leaves the hollow it becomes a lot harder to recover.

choices.label: Fight the remaining goblins
Cut off the runner before it reaches the gap
Knock the far gap entrance down to block escape
Shout to scatter the remaining grunts

stages.text: The iron is recovered. Most of the goblins have scattered into the forest, and the hollow sits empty except for their leavings and the faint smell of campfire smoke. You haul the iron out in armfuls — it's heavier than it looks.

choices.label: Carry the iron back to Gruff in one trip
Stack it efficiently to make fewer trips
Check the iron for damage before returning it

stages.text: You return the iron to Gruff. He grins and presses a bag of coins into your hand, then adds a militia blade from his wall — one he made himself, he says, and it's better than it looks.

quests.title: The Miller's Missing Son
quests.description: The miller's son went to deliver grain and never came back. The trail leads toward the forest.

stages.text: Miller Hobb wrings his flour-stained hands and hasn't stopped since yesterday morning. 'Tarin took the grain to the trapper's hut two days ago — it's only an hour's walk.' His voice breaks on the last word. 'His cart is still on the road. The horse came back alone.'

choices.label: Examine the abandoned cart for clues
Question neighbors who use that road regularly
Read the road for tracks leaving the cart
Follow the road immediately before the trail goes cold

stages.text: You find tracks near the cart: one set human, one set wolf, and then signs of a scramble where the human ran off the road and into the brush. Somewhere further in, branches are broken at about head-height for a running boy.

choices.label: Follow the wolf tracks to find where they led
Follow the broken branches to track the boy
Call out for Tarin carefully

stages.text: A timber wolf is nosing at a thorn thicket where something is hiding. The wolf hasn't attacked yet, but it's circling tighter each pass, and whatever is in the thicket is running out of time to stay hidden.

choices.label: Draw the wolf away from the thicket
Charge the wolf head-on to scare it off
Fight the timber wolf
Use a distraction to redirect the wolf's attention

stages.text: Tarin is alive in the thicket, frightened but uninjured, when a bandit scout emerges from behind a fallen oak with a blade already drawn. 'The boy saw our camp,' the scout says flatly. 'He stays with us.'

choices.label: Fight the bandit scout
Bluff that there are more of you close behind
Rush the scout before it can position
Circle wide to flank while it focuses on you

stages.text: The scout is dealt with and Tarin stumbles to his feet, clutching your arm the whole walk back. He is shaking and extremely talkative, which is, under the circumstances, a very good sign.

choices.label: Carry Tarin on your back through the rough ground
Navigate the safest route back to the road
Keep Tarin calm and talking on the way back

stages.text: Hobb bursts into tears when Tarin appears in the doorway. The whole mill smells of celebration baking within an hour. He presses a leather vest and all the coin he has on him into your hands before you can refuse either.

quests.title: Evil in the Shrine
quests.description: A wandering monk barely escaped a forest shrine. The dead are walking inside and something worse is below.

stages.text: A traveling monk clutches a broken holy symbol with both hands and refuses to sit down. 'I barely escaped. The dead walk in daylight inside those walls and there is a light in the lower chamber that should not be there.' The shrine is three miles into the Whispering Woods, and the monk says the door is still open behind him.

choices.label: Ask the monk to describe the lower chamber
Ask what drove the dead to rise here
Persuade the monk to draw you a rough map
Head for the shrine before dark

stages.text: The shrine reeks of decay, and the floor of the entrance hall is strewn with scattered bones and toppled votives. A staircase at the far end descends into a green-tinged light. In the upper chamber, two alcoves have been broken open, and the dust on the floor shows recent movement.

choices.label: Search the upper shrine for clues before descending
Listen at the staircase for sounds from below
Move quickly through the upper chamber to the stairs
Secure the upper chamber before going deeper

stages.text: Skeletons lurch from the alcoves — two of them, armed with whatever was buried with them, slow but relentless. Behind them a third rises from the floor itself, pieces assembling from the scattered bones on the tiles.

choices.label: Fight the skeletons
Smash the still-assembling skeleton before it rises
Weave between them to reach the staircase
Find the binding point and break it to drop all three

stages.text: A dusty journal on the upper shelf names the relic in the lower chamber: a cursed phylactery that was sealed here centuries ago and must not be disturbed. Someone has already disturbed it. The green light from below pulses brighter as you read.

choices.label: Descend the stairs ready for a fight
Read further to understand what you're about to face
Go carefully, one step at a time
Prepare a holy ward before descending

stages.text: In the lower chamber, a cursed priest floats above an altar surrounded by the broken seal of the phylactery, its arms spread wide, its voice a continuous low note that vibrates in your chest. The phylactery sits at the altar's centre — intact, malevolent, and waiting.

choices.label: Fight the cursed priest
Destroy the relic on the altar to cut its power
Shatter the altar itself to break the binding
Re-seal the phylactery using the journal's method

stages.text: The dark energy dissipates. The shrine falls silent for the first time in decades, the green light winks out, and the phylactery crumbles to cold ash. You find a plain iron ring in the debris — old enough to have been here from the beginning.

quests.title: The White Stag of Whispers
quests.description: A silver-white stag has been leading hunters into the deep forest and none of them have come back the same.

stages.text: A trapper points deeper into the trees with a finger that shakes just slightly. 'I saw it between the fog and moonlight — white as new linen, antlers like bare winter branches. Three hunters followed it last season. Two came back without their gear and with nothing to say about it. The third didn't come back.' He pauses. 'I need to know what's in there.'

choices.label: Ask the trapper for everything the two hunters said
Ask what direction the stag usually appears from
Ask what the trapper himself wants from this
Follow the stag's last known trail immediately

stages.text: Silver hoofprints weave through brambles into a grove where the air is unnaturally still and the trees have grown in tight concentric rings rather than the natural scatter of the rest of the forest. The stag's trail is visible but never quite where you are looking directly.

choices.label: Read the grove's signs and tree rings for history
Follow the trail exactly as it runs
Sense the grove's intent before moving deeper
Press straight through to the centre of the rings

stages.text: You realize the stag is bait — the trail has been looping you inward, and from above you can now see the grove is a trap. A thorn owlbear circles the outer ring, driving prey inward, waiting for the moment something is sufficiently lost. It has spotted you.

choices.label: Ready yourself and fight the owlbear
Back out using the grove's own ring structure
Sprint for the outer ring before it can close
Climb the densest thorn cluster where it can't follow

stages.text: The owlbear is down. In the silence that follows, the white stag appears at the grove's edge — present, very real, and watching you with amber eyes that contain a question that has nothing to do with hunting. It moves toward a half-buried stone near the centre of the inner ring and stops.

choices.label: Approach the stag slowly and carefully
Read the inscription on the buried stone
Stay still and let the stag approach on its own terms
Speak to the stag as if it understands you

stages.text: The stag lowers its head toward the buried stone, then lifts it and holds your gaze for a moment before stepping backward into the trees without sound. The stone conceals a sealed alcove, and inside is a carefully wrapped supply of greater healing. Whatever the grove is, it was not empty before this hunt.

choices.label: Recover the supplies and mark the grove's location
Leave the grove quickly before anything else appears
Take a moment to understand what just happened

stages.text: The stag is gone. The grove is quiet. You carry something out of there that isn't just the potion — a sense that the forest has its own logic, and that occasionally it decides to help.

quests.title: The Bandit King's Toll
quests.description: A self-styled Bandit King has set up a toll at a road junction and is extorting every merchant who passes.

stages.text: A merchant at the crossroads junction is furious and frightened in equal measure. 'That villain took half my goods and had the nerve to call it a toll — said the road belongs to him now, from the third milestone to the burned inn.' He unfolds a map and stabs a finger at a camp symbol. 'There are a dozen of them and he has a proper sword.'

choices.label: Study the map to plan an approach
Ask other merchants what they've seen at the camp
Estimate the camp's defensive layout from the map
Head for the camp before more tolls are collected

stages.text: You find the camp: a dozen tents, a cookfire, guards on rotation. The captain sits at the fire eating a merchant's confiscated meal with the relaxed posture of someone who has never been seriously challenged. The guards rotate lazily but regularly, and there is a pattern to it.

choices.label: Challenge the captain publicly and loudly
Study the rotation to find the gap in coverage
Poison the communal cookpot with a sleep draft
Sneak to the captain's tent while the fire distracts the guards

stages.text: Three bandits block your path before you reach the captain — they've noticed you're not paying the toll. They look bored and very willing to make an example of someone.

choices.label: Fight through the bandits
Scatter them with aggression before they can coordinate
Use the camp layout to split them apart
Bluff that you have authority from the crown

stages.text: The captain rises when he hears the commotion, drawing a proper sword from a proper scabbard. He moves well and has clearly done this before. 'I like to keep things impersonal,' he says, 'but here we are.'

choices.label: Fight the bandit captain
Draw him away from his remaining men
Find his defensive weakness in the first exchange
Break his nerve with a precise first strike

stages.text: The captain falls and the camp scatters. You collect the confiscated goods and the captain's own coin purse, then spend a productive hour dismantling his toll post and burning the camp's supply of rope. The road sign goes back up pointing the right direction.

choices.label: Recover the merchants' confiscated goods
Dismantle the toll infrastructure permanently
Leave a visible warning for any remaining bandits

stages.text: The road is free again. The first merchant through offers you half his remaining coin and a fine steel dagger he'd hidden in his boot. You take the dagger. The road belongs to travelers again.

quests.title: The Buried Bells
quests.description: Travelers hear church bells beneath the broken road at night. Graves from a lost chapel are opening.

stages.text: An old drover won't touch his mule and hasn't since Tuesday. 'You hear them too, don't you? Bells from under the dirt. Something is digging upward.' He marks an X on his knee with a thumb. 'Half-collapsed headstones around a sunken crypt, three miles past the second ford. The bells start after midnight.'

choices.label: Ask the drover about the crypt's history
Ask who else has heard the bells
Study the route to approach safely
Head for the crypt before dark to see it in daylight

stages.text: Half-collapsed headstones surround a sunken chapel crypt that has settled two feet into the road over the decades. Fresh footprints and claw marks crisscross the mud — both human-sized and something that drags rather than walks. The crypt door is ajar.

choices.label: Follow the human footprints
Examine the claw drag marks for origin
Listen at the crypt door for sounds inside
Open the crypt door quietly

stages.text: A grave robber crouches inside the entry chamber with a lantern and a bag already half-full of chapel relics. He gasps when he sees you and immediately starts explaining that he was just looking. Behind him, the inner door moves — and no one is on the other side pushing it.

choices.label: Grab the grave robber before he can flee with the relics
Fight the grave robber
Let him go and focus on what is behind the inner door
Use the robber as a distraction to get through the inner door

stages.text: You slip inside and find bells hanging over a chamber of broken coffins. A pilgrim revenant rises from the furthest one with soil still falling from its burial robes, moving toward the bell rope with the single-minded intent of something that has a job to do and means to do it.

choices.label: Fight the revenant
Cut the bell rope before it reaches it
Recite the chapel's dedication to interrupt the rite
Collapse the bell mechanism from above

stages.text: The bells fall silent and the revenant disperses. The crypt is quiet except for the sound of settling stone. Among the broken coffins and recovered relics is a set of chainmail that clearly belonged to someone who was buried in it and is no longer in a position to need it.

choices.label: Recover the chainmail and search for anything else salvageable
Seal the inner door so nothing else can ring those bells
Offer a blessing over the broken coffins before leaving

stages.text: The bells finally fall silent. Dawn breaks over the road and the oppressive chill lifts from the graves. The chainmail is yours — and the road is a little safer than it was before breakfast.

quests.title: Fire Below
quests.description: Fire elementals have risen in Ironhold's deep shafts and the miners cannot work.

stages.text: Foreman Thordak tugs his braided beard and does not look at all like someone who has slept recently. 'The deep shafts are overrun. Fire elementals — not just heat and light but things with intent, breaking support timbers, blocking the ore face.' He slaps a map of the lower levels down. 'I need them gone. I need the deep shafts workable by the end of the week or we miss our contract and I miss my livelihood.'

choices.label: Study the map for the elemental concentration points
Ask Thordak what triggered the infestation
Volunteer to go down and assess in person
Ask what equipment the miners used when they retreated

stages.text: Heat hits you like a physical wall when you descend past the second junction. The tunnel glows orange-red from somewhere below, and you can see why the miners stopped here — the air shimmers, the timber props are scorched, and something moves in the heat haze that is not a trick of the light.

choices.label: Press deeper through the heat along the main shaft
Take the cooler side passage to approach the elementals from below
Move carefully and read the heat distribution
Check for the source of the ignition before engaging

stages.text: A magma elemental rises from the molten rock pooled at the tunnel junction — not large, but hot enough to crack stone with its passage. It has not noticed you yet but is oriented toward the timber supports at the ore face. If it reaches those, the shaft collapses.

choices.label: Fight the magma elemental
Draw it away from the timber supports with movement
Douse its heat source at the tunnel junction
Strike it from behind before it reaches the timbers

stages.text: The side passage leads behind the elemental nest. You find the source: a cracked containment rune carved into the deepest ore face, the seal broken by someone who either didn't know what it was or didn't care. The rune is still partially active — fixing it or destroying it will end the infestation, but it requires precision.

choices.label: Destroy the rune completely with brute force
Repair the containment seal from memory and guesswork
Dispel the rune using its own resonance pattern
Carefully chip away the active sections without triggering a collapse

stages.text: The rune goes cold. The heat in the tunnels drops almost immediately — not all the way, not yet, but the shimmering stops and the elementals lose coherence. Thordak is at the third junction when you come up, which means he followed you down partway and won't admit it.

choices.label: Show Thordak the broken rune and explain what happened
Help Thordak clear the cooled debris for the miners
Advise Thordak on preventing re-activation

stages.text: The fires die down. The mines cool. Thordak presses a war axe into your hands — from the mine armoury, he says, kept for situations exactly like this one, which it was not, but you're not going to argue.

quests.title: Sabotage in the Deep
quests.description: Someone is cracking support beams in Ironhold's lower mines, and the foreman thinks it's deliberate.

stages.text: A stern quartermaster lowers his voice to the register of someone who has been wrong before and does not want to be wrong again. 'This isn't bad luck. Someone is cracking beams in the lower shafts — three in a week, always near the ore caches, always at shift change when the count is off.' He slides a map across the table. 'I need to know who and why before anyone else gets buried.'

choices.label: Examine the damaged support beams for tool marks
Question the miners who were on shift during each incident
Review the ore count discrepancies for a pattern
Go down into the lower shafts to see the damage in person

stages.text: The tool marks on the damaged beams are deliberate — not the work of someone in a hurry but of someone who knew exactly where to strike to weaken the structure without triggering an immediate collapse. Professional sabotage, not vandalism. And the ore count shortfalls correspond to the same shifts every time.

choices.label: Trace the ore shortfalls to a specific shift crew
Identify which shafts are being avoided by experienced miners
Follow the least-used tunnel route to find where things converge
Set a watch on the compromised beams overnight

stages.text: A slag hound prowls the tunnels near the sabotaged section — not wild but placed, chained just long enough to guard the approach to wherever the stolen ore is being staged. It starts barking the moment it scents you, which is not a good omen for your cover.

choices.label: Fight the slag hound
Silence the hound before it finishes the alert
Find another route around the hound's chain radius
Wait for the barking to be dismissed as normal

stages.text: Past the hound, you find the staging area: a collapsed gallery being used as a storage cache, three weeks of stolen ore in sacks, and a deserter lancer arriving with a wagon to make the first real collection. He is not surprised to see someone here. He was told it was possible.

choices.label: Fight the deserter lancer
Collapse the tunnel entrance before he can load and go
Disarm and subdue him before he can use the wagon as cover
Negotiate his informants in exchange for not reporting him

stages.text: The saboteur is in custody. The quartermaster arrives down the shaft within twenty minutes, which suggests he was waiting close by. He goes through the recovered ore with the professional calm of someone who was right all along and takes no satisfaction from it at all.

choices.label: Give the full account of what you found and how
Help secure the cache for recovery
Provide the quartermaster with the full evidence chain

stages.text: The sabotage ring is broken. Ironhold's foremen drag the stolen ore back to the surface with a grim efficiency that suggests this is not the first time the mines have been robbed and won't be the last. The quartermaster gives you an iron shield from the armoury — solid, plain, and exactly what it claims to be.

quests.title: The Hanging Tree Oath
quests.description: A murdered pilgrim's ghost manifests each night at the crossroads hanging tree, and a thief profits from the panic.

stages.text: A caravan master won't camp near the hanging tree. 'Every night a dead pilgrim appears at the rope, arms raised, and anyone who looks directly faints and wakes up missing their coin.' He's lost three guards this week to unexplained absences and is done with supernatural interference on his profit margin.

choices.label: Ask about the pilgrim's identity from local records
Listen to what witnesses actually describe at sunrise
Keep watch near the tree at the right time
Ask why the guards haven't come back

stages.text: You establish that the manifestation happens within the first hour after midnight, that it has a consistent pattern — the pilgrim rises, raises its arms, and the fainting begins — and that a masked cutpurse has been following the crowd of onlookers afterward and relieving the unconscious of their valuables. Two separate problems with one shared schedule.

choices.label: Position yourself to catch the cutpurse in the act
Research the pilgrim's death to understand the ghost
Prepare a ward against the fainting effect
Alert the caravan guards to what is really happening

stages.text: At sunset, a translucent pilgrim manifests beneath the branches with arms raised, and the masked cutpurse moves through the crowd of onlookers toward a target. The ghost's presence radiates cold intent that is designed to disorient. You are currently one of the few people still standing upright.

choices.label: Run down the cutpurse before they can work the crowd
Address the spirit directly to interrupt the manifestation
Focus and resist the disorientation to stay functional
Shout a warning to keep the crowd from fainting

stages.text: The cutpurse is dealt with, but the spirit remains — the ghost of the pilgrim is still here, still manifesting, still raising its arms in the gesture of someone who has something unfinished and cannot stop pointing at it. The noose creaks above with no wind.

choices.label: Touch the hanging rope to sense what is anchored there
Read the rope for evidence of the original crime
Speak aloud the name of the guilty party
Cut the noose down to release the binding

stages.text: You see the truth: the pilgrim was murdered for a relic and hanged here to hide the crime as a suicide. The spirit needs the name spoken at the site where it happened, which is exactly here and exactly now.

choices.label: Speak the name of the guilty and the nature of the crime
Record the full account so the truth is in writing
Complete the rite of acknowledgement at the tree

stages.text: The revenant bows its head and dissolves into pale light. The crossroads sleeps easier now. The shadow gem was hidden in the cutpurse's satchel — it belonged to the murdered pilgrim, which is why the ghost kept pointing.

quests.title: The Smuggler's Wagon
quests.description: A suspicious wagon with a false floor has been seen at the crossroads. The cargo inside is still alive.

stages.text: A traveling cleric tugs your sleeve and will not let go. 'That wagon — I heard scratching from inside, and not from cargo. The driver threatened me when I looked too closely and gave me a silver to forget about it.' The wagon is still at the crossroads, driver circling with proprietary ease. Something inside it is desperate.

choices.label: Observe the wagon from a distance to establish a pattern
Ask the crossroads innkeeper if they've seen this wagon before
Read the wagon for signs of false compartments
Create a distraction to draw the driver away from the wagon

stages.text: With the driver occupied, you get a close look at the wagon. The floor boards are newer than the rest and don't flex the way load-bearing wood should — there is a compartment beneath. The scratching is louder here, and there is a muffled sound that is unmistakably mammalian and very unhappy.

choices.label: Force the false floor open immediately
Find the release mechanism without making noise
Identify what is being transported from the sounds
Check whether the driver is alone first

stages.text: Under the false floor: caged animals — rare beasts meant for an underground fighting pit, each one worth more frightened than healthy. The driver has noticed you and is walking over with the patient expression of someone who does this for a living and has seen every kind of hero.

choices.label: Confront the driver directly with what you found
Free the animals before the driver reaches you
Stand between the driver and the wagon
Call witnesses from the crossroads crowd

stages.text: The driver draws a blade. 'Mind your own business, hero,' he says, in the tone of someone who has said this many times to many people and is thoroughly bored of the conversation.

choices.label: Fight the rogue mercenary driver
Disarm him before he can commit to the attack
Use the crowd of witnesses to weaken his nerve
Feint high and take him low

stages.text: The driver is defeated. The animals are freed and a grateful druid who was apparently watching the whole thing from the crossroads crowd emerges to thank you at some length. The cages are broken open, the wagon is impounded, and the driver is someone else's problem now.

choices.label: Help the druid release the remaining caged animals
Question the driver about who hired him
Accept the druid's thanks and the crowd's attention

stages.text: The smuggler is defeated. You free the caged beasts and a grateful druid rewards you with an antidote kit from her own pack — the kind you need when things in cages bite back.

quests.title: Lanterns in the Mist
quests.description: The will-o'-wisps that once guided ferrymen now lure them into the deep water instead.

stages.text: An old ferryman stares into the marsh with the expression of a man who has lost something he trusted. 'They were once lanterns on safe water. Every ferryman on this route knew to follow them.' He points at the mist. 'Now they lead boats off the bank and into the deep. Three skiffs gone in a month. One ferryman didn't come back.' Something changed the lights.

choices.label: Ask the ferryman when the behaviour first changed
Study the lights' movement pattern from the bank
Follow the lights carefully from a safe distance
Ask what happened to the ferryman who didn't return

stages.text: The will-o'-wisps lead you from hummock to hummock through paths that are technically passable but are clearly designed to disorient. Bones lie beneath the reeds — old ones and new ones — and the lights pulse in a pattern that mimics the old safety signals but in a sequence no ferryman would recognise.

choices.label: Decode the false signal sequence to understand the manipulation
Step carefully and test each hummock before committing weight
Follow the bones to find where the lights have led before
Call out to the lights to see if they respond to voice

stages.text: A marsh ghoul erupts from the mud, drawn by the lights and the smell of the living. It has been following the wisps and feeding on whatever they bring in — a symbiotic relationship that neither party chose but both have benefited from. It attacks without ceremony.

choices.label: Fight the marsh ghoul
Use the wisps' own light to blind the ghoul
Drive the ghoul back into the deep water
Draw the ghoul away from the solid ground before engaging

stages.text: The lights gather around a sunken skiff at the deepest navigable point. You realise the dead ferrymen were never properly recovered — the wisps have been anchored here by the grief of drowning deaths that were never acknowledged. The lights are not malicious, but they are trapped in a memory of loss that makes them dangerous.

choices.label: Fight the will-o'-wisp to break the anchor
Recover the drowned remains from the skiff
Speak the ferrymen's names into the water
Use a proper ferryman's lantern signal to redirect the wisps

stages.text: Once the dead are honoured, the lights settle into still lantern-flame and drift to the bank's edge — the original guiding positions, the ones the ferrymen knew. The marsh route is safe again, and the lights burn steady and true.

choices.label: Return to the ferryman and tell him what you found
Retrieve the hydra scale from the sunken skiff's cargo
Mark the corrected light positions for the ferrying guild

stages.text: The lights settle into their proper positions, steady and faithful again. The hydra scale was in the sunken cargo, carried by the last ferryman who knew the original route — left where it fell, waiting.

quests.title: The Fen Witch
quests.description: Undead sightings grow worse by the day. The source is the crooked tower at the marsh's heart.

stages.text: Undead sightings grow worse by the day, and the marsh stinks of death in a way that moves against the wind. A crooked tower rises from the fen's centre — lights in its upper windows at hours when no fire should be burning. A retired warden says plainly: 'Whatever is in that tower is what's sending them. The tower has to fall.'

choices.label: Research the tower's history and its occupant
Scout the tower's approaches from the treeline
Ask the warden what she knows about the witch inside
Move toward the tower before the light changes

stages.text: The tower's entrance is guarded. Animated suits of armor stand at the door — two visible, likely more inside. A window on the second level is dark and unlatched, an oversight or a test. The front door is heavy iron, sealed with a mechanism that requires a key or considerable persuasion.

choices.label: Fight through the armor at the front entrance
Find a window to climb on the tower's blind side
Study the armor's patrol pattern for a window
Force the iron door directly

stages.text: Inside, dark familiars hiss and swoop between the shelves of a library that smells of old magic and bad intentions. The stairs spiral upward, and something moves on every landing. The familiars are small but quick and they will alert the witch if they can.

choices.label: Fight the dark familiars
Move through the library without disturbing the familiars
Find the familiar's anchor object and break it
Ascend quickly before they can cohere into a real threat

stages.text: You pull yourself through into a library of forbidden texts on the third level. The witch is above — you can hear her moving, and the tower itself seems to respond to her mood, creaking and settling in ways that have nothing to do with wind. The access to the roof level is a trapdoor above the final stair.

choices.label: Climb through the trapdoor immediately
Read the texts near the trapdoor for tactical information
Listen through the trapdoor to assess the witch's position
Open the trapdoor fast enough that she cannot prepare

stages.text: The Fen Witch awaits on the roof with lightning crackling between her fingers and an expression of mild professional interest. 'You made it further than the last three,' she says, which is not quite a compliment. She attacks without further preamble.

choices.label: Fight the Fen Witch
Use the roof structure to limit her range of movement
Close the gap before she can build a full casting
Match her confidence with equal certainty

stages.text: The witch crumbles to ash. Her tower groans and begins to sink. You escape with her staff, which is still crackling with something it hasn't finished saying, and the undead in the marsh stop walking within the hour.

quests.title: Ashes Over Sunspire
quests.description: Cinder cultists are operating a fire beacon on the lower peaks to guide a drake attack.

stages.text: A sun-priest shields a brazier from drifting ash with her body. 'These embers are no omen — they're not from Sunspire's fires and they're not from the peaks. They taste of something deliberately burnt.' She holds a handful to the light. 'Someone is burning signal materials up there. Specific ratios. This is a beacon compound.'

choices.label: Identify the beacon compound composition
Study the ash drift direction to locate the source
Scout the lower peak approaches for the beacon position
Alert the city garrison before moving on the cultists

stages.text: Burnt prayer flags and scorched footprints lead to a ruined watchpost overlooking the eastern approach — a position with clear line of sight to both the city and the drake flight paths. A ritual circle of ash has been maintained here for at least three days, and it is currently active.

choices.label: Scout the watchpost from below before committing to approach
Read the ritual circle's composition and purpose
Charge the ritual circle before the beacon is completed
Observe the number of cultists before engaging

stages.text: You see cultists feeding flame to a beacon while an ash drake circles overhead in a holding pattern — it's waiting for a signal that the beacon will give. The cult leader stands at the circle's centre with a sealed flask of beacon compound. The drake will commit its attack run when the flask is opened.

choices.label: Destroy the beacon before the flask is opened
Shoot down the drake before it can receive the signal
Fight the cinder cultists at the circle
Take the cult leader out before the flask is opened

stages.text: The cult leader falls but the beacon fires are still lit, and the ash drake breaks its holding pattern and begins its attack run without waiting for the flask signal — it's been circling long enough to become impatient. The beacon needs to be extinguished before the drake commits fully.

choices.label: Fight the ash drake
Smother the beacon fires before the drake locks in
Drive the drake off with a concentrated distraction
Use the watchpost structure to break the drake's dive angle

stages.text: The beacon collapses in sparks. Sunspire's defenders cheer from the walls below. The cult leader's sealed flask contains the beacon's activation key and complete instructions — including the location of the drake nesting site they've been working with, which the garrison is very interested in.

choices.label: Deliver the flask and documents to the garrison command
Dismantle the ritual circle permanently
Scout the drake nesting site location while you have it

stages.text: The beacon collapses in sparks. Sunspire's defenders cheer from the walls and the garrison commander gives you a flame visor from the vanguard stores — the kind worn by people who walk into fires they started themselves and walk back out again.

quests.title: Ignavoth Must Fall
quests.description: Ignavoth the Ancient has awakened. If the wyrm destroys Sunspire, the entire region falls.

stages.text: Archmage Liora stands before the council with the stillness of someone delivering news they've been carrying for days. 'Ignavoth has awakened. The tremors in Dragon Peak are not geological — they are the movement of something very old deciding it is time to act.' She sets a sealed expedition order on the table. 'Someone has to go up there. The question is whether you understand what that means.'

choices.label: Ask the Archmage everything known about Ignavoth
Study the Dragon Peak approach routes for weaknesses
Ask what happened to the last expedition that attempted this
Accept immediately and request the best available equipment

stages.text: The climb up Dragon Peak is brutal in ways the maps did not convey. Drakes circle overhead in a defensive pattern, the air smells of sulphur and hot stone, and the path narrows at three points where a moment's inattention has a very long consequence. Something has been maintaining this approach as a killing ground for a very long time.

choices.label: Navigate the narrowing path sections with complete focus
Study the drake patrol pattern to move through its gaps
Identify which parts of the path were deliberately prepared
Move steadily and accept that speed is the only cover available

stages.text: Near the summit, a flame knight bars the path — a human enslaved by the dragon's will, his eyes reflecting heat-light from the peak above. He attacks with the mechanical precision of something that has been given one instruction and has spent weeks perfecting the execution of it.

choices.label: Fight the flame knight
Try to break the dragon's enchantment with a counter-working
Find the enchantment's anchor point and sever it
Use the knight's rigidity against him in the exchange

stages.text: The knight falls. Freed from the dragon's control, he blinks with the expression of someone waking from a very long sleep. 'Thank you,' he says. 'The dragon controls everything up here. You'll feel it before you see it — a weight, like being watched by something very old that has always gotten what it wanted.' He gestures upward. 'Go now, while it hasn't recalibrated for you.'

choices.label: Ask the freed knight everything he observed while controlled
Read the peak approaches for the lair entrance position
Enter the lair before the dragon knows you cleared its guardian
Prepare at the lair entrance before entering

stages.text: Ignavoth is colossal. Gold and bones carpet the cavern in equal measure, and the ancient wyrm opens one eye — the size of a cartwheel, older than Sunspire, more patient than anything you have faced — and the weight of its attention is a physical pressure. 'You are very small,' it says, in a voice like mountains disagreeing, 'to have come so far alone.'

choices.label: Answer it without showing fear
Assess the lair for tactical position while it speaks
Read the dragon's posture for when it will stop talking
Hold your ground under the full weight of its attention

stages.text: IGNAVOTH THE ANCIENT rises to full height. The cavern fills with heat and the sound of something ancient drawing breath. Whatever you said or did in those last moments before the fight begins, the accounting starts now.

choices.label: FIGHT IGNAVOTH
Find the killing angle before it completes its first strike
Use the cavern's columns for cover and close the distance
Meet the ancient's first strike and survive it

stages.text: The dragon crashes to the cavern floor. The mountain trembles. You stand victorious — Dragon's Heart still warm in your hand, the ancient wyrm still, the cavern silent except for settling stone. Sunspire stands. Whatever comes next, it will not come from the sky in fire.

quests.title: Night Watch at the Palisade
quests.description: The village palisade gate keeps banging open at night. Something is testing it.

stages.text: The gate guard's eyes are ringed with exhaustion, his knuckles raw from hammering the latch back into place each morning. 'Something hits it every night around the second watch — not hard enough to break through, but hard enough to know it's thinking about it.' Paw prints ring the post, long and deliberate.

choices.label: Examine the gate for signs of damage
Study the paw prints in the mud
Ask the guard what he heard and when

stages.text: You take up a position behind the post as darkness settles over Thornwall. An hour passes, then another — and then a shape moves along the tree line, low and deliberate, testing the shadows for movement. It circles once, then again, each pass a little closer.

choices.label: Move to intercept it before it reaches the gate
Hold still and let it commit to the approach
Watch the pattern and predict where it will strike

stages.text: A second wolf emerges from the brush and begins howling to signal the pack. If the pack rallies now, one gate guard and a hired hand won't be enough to hold the palisade until morning.

choices.label: Silence the howling wolf immediately
Chase it off with a torch from the gate
Circle wide and cut it off before it can call

stages.text: The pack's lead wolf breaks from the trees and charges the gate head-on, shouldering into the timber hard enough to crack one post at the base. The guard shouts from the wall. This is the push — stop it here, or nothing holds.

choices.label: Fight the lead wolf
Drop a burning brand in its path
Drive it back against the wall with your weight
Slip sideways and redirect its charge into the post

stages.text: The pack retreats when the lead wolf falls. The guard is already hammering the cracked post before the wolves reach the tree line again, and when he finishes he stands there looking at the quiet forest like a man who has earned a full night's sleep for the first time all week.

choices.label: Help reinforce the cracked post before dawn
Scout the tree line to confirm the pack has gone
Advise the guard on better nighttime watch rotations

stages.text: The village wakes to an intact gate and a guard who has finally slept. You have earned an honest morning in Thornwall, and a bow from the armoury kept for exactly this kind of debt.

quests.title: The Ratcatcher's Tithe
quests.description: The grain stores are infested, and the professional ratcatcher hasn't been seen in three days.

stages.text: The storekeeper's hands are shaking as she shows you the gnawed sacks — not just one or two, but all of them, chewed through from the inside and the grain pooling like sand. 'The ratcatcher took the job four days ago,' she says. 'Haven't seen him since. Haven't heard him either, which is worse.'

choices.label: Follow gnaw marks to find their source
Listen beneath the floorboards for sounds
Ask neighbors if they saw where he went

stages.text: A trail of disturbed flour leads beneath the raised floorboards, where the ratcatcher apparently crawled in and — based on the drag marks and the silence — never came out on his own. Something below shifts. Then several somethings. Then too many.

choices.label: Pry up the floorboards by force
Slip under the boards to search quietly
Count the sounds to gauge the colony size

stages.text: You find the ratcatcher wedged into a corner beneath the boards, very much alive and very much done with his career. He hasn't moved in two days, surrounded by a dire rat swarm that has taken a particular interest in him and is now taking an interest in you.

choices.label: Haul the ratcatcher out and seal the gap
Lure the swarm away before grabbing him
Slip through and pull him out quietly

stages.text: The ratcatcher is safe but the swarm is still down here, and if it isn't dealt with now it will simply recolonise the stores within the week. The boards are up, the light is in, and the swarm is angry.

choices.label: Fight the swarm directly
Scatter them with fire and smoke
Drive them toward the open drain and block it
Use the ratcatcher's abandoned bait to lead them out

stages.text: The swarm breaks. The storekeeper helps you nail boards over the worst gaps while the ratcatcher sits outside looking at the sky with the expression of a man reconsidering his profession entirely.

choices.label: Help seal the floor gaps so it can't happen again
Check all remaining sacks for damage
Reassure the storekeeper the stores are safe

stages.text: The stores are saved, the ratcatcher is alive, and Thornwall owes you a debt. The hunter's charm was the ratcatcher's — he says you've earned it more than he has, and he means it.

quests.title: The Flour Trail Ambush
quests.description: A baker's flour deliveries keep vanishing on the forest road, and the drivers are too scared to say what they saw.

stages.text: The baker's apprentice won't meet your eyes. 'He told me not to say anything, but someone needs to do something.' A flour trail leads from the market road into the brush, thin but unbroken, as if the sacks were dragged and leaking the whole way. The drivers say it happens near the old tree at the road bend.

choices.label: Follow the flour trail into the brush
Question the drivers to get the full story
Examine the ambush site for clues
Get there before the next delivery is due

stages.text: The trail leads to a crude stockpile behind a collapsed wood-gatherer's hut — at least six stolen sacks, a gnawed rope, and several sets of small booted footprints circling the whole area. Goblins, probably, but organized ones with a stash and a pattern.

choices.label: Search the hut for more clues about the operation
Study the footprints to estimate how many there are
Set up to observe the stockpile without being seen

stages.text: You spot the lookout too late — a goblin grunt perched in the big beech above the stockpile, already raising a bone horn to its lips. If it calls for the others, your window closes entirely.

choices.label: Sprint and silence it before it blows
Throw something to knock it from the tree
Startle it so it blows too early

stages.text: Three goblin grunts charge from behind the hut with crude blades up, shouting over each other. They are small and fierce and completely convinced that this flour belongs to them.

choices.label: Fight the goblin grunts
Topple the flour stash onto them
Divide and isolate them before they can coordinate
Drive them off with noise and aggression

stages.text: The goblins scatter. You haul the stolen flour sacks back toward the road, two at a time, while the stockpile slowly collapses behind you. The baker's apprentice is waiting at the road junction, and her relief is very loud.

choices.label: Carry all the sacks back yourself
