Set a warning at the ambush site to deter future attempts
Teach the drivers what to watch for next time

stages.text: The baker rewards you with the coin he'd been saving for a real guard and a thief's stiletto he found wedged in one of the returned sacks — apparently someone lost it there before you arrived.

quests.title: The Old Chapel Reliquary
quests.description: A wandering priest says a holy reliquary was left in an abandoned chapel. The dead inside won't let it go.

stages.text: The wandering priest is thin-boned and apologetic about the whole thing. 'I left the reliquary in the chapel when the village was abandoned — I had to carry the children, you understand. I have prayed on this for nine years.' The chapel is half a mile east, off the old road, and no one from Thornwall goes near it.

choices.label: Ask the priest exactly where the reliquary was placed
Ask why no one from the village will go near it
Volunteer before he can finish explaining the danger
Head to the chapel now

stages.text: The chapel is still standing, which is more than you expected. The door has been nailed shut from the outside, with fresh nails over older ones — someone has been repeating this task for years. From inside comes a faint sound, rhythmic and hollow, like knuckles tapping on stone.

choices.label: Force the door open
Find a window or gap to observe through first
Listen to determine what's making the sound

stages.text: Inside, the pews have been overturned and stacked into a rough barricade around the altar. A restless skeleton lurches from behind the pews the moment light enters — still wearing what were once good clothes, still clutching a rusted offering bowl, still very much here.

choices.label: Fight the skeleton
Keep the offering bowl between you and it
Throw the pews aside to clear a path to the altar
Recite a prayer to interrupt the restless state

stages.text: The skeleton is past, and the altar is ahead. The reliquary sits exactly where the priest described — under the lectern, wrapped in old cloth, intact. But the floorboards between you and the altar are rotten, and the weight of all this old grief is palpable.

choices.label: Move carefully across the rotted boards
Brace the boards with a pew timber first
Test each board before committing your weight

stages.text: You have the reliquary. It is heavier than expected and warm, as though it remembers being carried with purpose. The chapel settles behind you, the tapping stops, and the quiet that follows feels genuinely peaceful for the first time in nine years.

choices.label: Bring it back to the priest immediately
Seal the chapel door behind you
Take a moment to make sure everything is truly quiet

stages.text: The priest weeps openly and presses the acolyte vestments from his own pack into your hands — everything he had left from the chapel, he says, and it belongs with someone who walked back through those doors.

quests.title: Gruff's Iron Oath
quests.description: Old Gruff made an oath on a battlefield once. Something has come to collect on it.

stages.text: Gruff hasn't spoken in two days, which everyone in Thornwall finds more alarming than his shouting. He sits by the forge staring at his hands. 'There's a thing waiting at the old oath-post in the eastern field,' he says finally. 'I made a promise on that field twenty years ago. Came to a head last night.' He looks up. 'I'm too old to keep it myself.'

choices.label: Ask Gruff what the oath was exactly
Press him for details about what's waiting
Agree to go without asking questions
Ask Gruff what he wants you to do when you get there

stages.text: The eastern field is quiet in the way fields shouldn't be — no birds, no wind, the grass all bent in the same direction away from the post. The oath-post stands in the centre, carved with battle-marks and bound with a broken sword hilt someone hammered in decades ago. There is something else here too, patient and watching.

choices.label: Approach the post directly
Circle the field to find where it's waiting
Study the post and the marks on it
Stand at the field edge and announce yourself

stages.text: A hobgoblin bruiser steps out of the long grass — old, battle-scarred, with Gruff's regimental mark burned into its left shoulder. It speaks in broken common: 'The oathsman said someone would come. Are you honouring it or breaking it?' The question hangs in the still air.

choices.label: Say you are honouring the oath on Gruff's behalf
Ask what honouring the oath requires
Study the hobgoblin's posture for its real intent
Hold your ground without speaking

stages.text: The hobgoblin's answer is a blade — it has been waiting twenty years for the oath to be kept, and it has decided the only way to keep it now is to test whether you are worth the promise Gruff made. It attacks with the patience of something that has been waiting a very long time.

choices.label: Fight the hobgoblin bruiser
Use the post as cover and fight defensively
Match its patience and wait for an opening
Attack first and don't give it room to breathe

stages.text: The hobgoblin falls back, bloodied but not broken. It sits down beside the post and looks at you for a long moment. Then it pulls the broken sword hilt from the post and holds it out to you. 'Oath kept,' it says, and something in the field releases.

choices.label: Take the hilt and say nothing
Ask if the hobgoblin needs anything before you go
Carry it back to Gruff immediately

stages.text: Gruff holds the hilt for a long time. He forges a militia blade from memory that night and leaves it outside your door before dawn — no note, no ceremony, but with the kind of care that only goes into something made for someone you respect.

quests.title: Roots of the Old Oak
quests.description: A trapper claims something beneath an ancient oak is poisoning the game trails.

stages.text: The trapper hasn't slept in three days and smells like he's been sitting in the same spot for most of them. He spreads a creased map across his knee and traces a shaking finger to a dark smear near the forest's heart. 'Nothing comes back from that oak,' he says. 'Not the rabbits, not the hounds, not even the quiet.' The bark of every tree within a quarter mile has turned the colour of dried blood.

choices.label: Examine the trapper's poison-blackened snares
Read the forest's mood before moving in
Plot the fastest route on the trapper's map
Head for the oak while there is still light

stages.text: The forest darkens as you move deeper, the canopy knitting shut overhead until the sky disappears. The ancient oak rises alone in a clearing stripped of grass, its roots writhing slowly beneath the topsoil like fingers finding purchase. Black sap weeps from every crack in its bark, threading into the soil in lines that kill whatever they touch.

choices.label: Force through the wall of thorns at the clearing edge
Study the root patterns for a safe approach
Move along the clearing's edge without disturbing it
Sense which direction the corruption is weakest

stages.text: A forest spider drops from the canopy the moment you reach the root line — enormous, its abdomen slick with the same black sap as the tree. It has nested among the corrupted roots, feeding on whatever the rot draws upward. Between you and the heart of the corruption, it spreads all eight legs wide and waits.

choices.label: Fight the spider
Drive it off using fire from a torch
Slip past it before it can close the gap
Mimic a larger predator to unsettle it

stages.text: The source of the rot is a swollen knot at the oak's base, the size of a millstone, weeping black pulp and pulsing with something like a slow, deliberate heartbeat. This is not disease — it is intent, ancient and patient, capable of ruining an entire valley one root at a time. It will keep spreading unless you end it here.

choices.label: Hack into the knot with your weapon
Find and sever the main feeding root
Cut away the outer rot layer by careful layer
Speak a warding against earthbound corruption

stages.text: The knot collapses inward, the pulsing stops, and the black sap runs dry all at once. Around you the roots settle into normal soil and the birds return — distant at first, then closer. The trapper is waiting at the clearing's edge when you emerge, and the relief on his face says everything that needs saying.

choices.label: Help drag the dead root mass off the trail
Mark the area safe on the trapper's map
Walk back to camp with the trapper

stages.text: Fresh air fills the grove for the first time in weeks. The trapper rewards you with trail leathers sewn for rough country — stitched, he says, for a debt he is very glad to finally pay.

quests.title: Silk and Moonlight
quests.description: A fletcher needs rare silk from the forest spiders to finish an order of master arrows.

stages.text: The fletcher turns a snapped bowstring over in her fingers with the focused disappointment of someone who expected better from the universe. 'Moonlit web silk,' she says without looking up, 'is the only thing strong enough for what I'm making. The spiders only spin it where the canopy lets the moon through. Go at dusk. Never at night.'

choices.label: Ask about the spiders' territory and behaviour
Study the broken bowstring under the light
Ask the fletcher how to harvest the silk cleanly
Head for the web grove before the light changes

stages.text: You find the web grove just as the light is changing — silver threads strung between the pines like a second canopy, each strand glowing faintly where the last sun catches it. The silk is extraordinary: strong, warm, completely without knots. But the spiders are still here, and they notice you the moment you step inside the grove's edge.

choices.label: Move slowly to avoid vibrating the threads
Identify which strands are load-bearing before touching any
Wait motionless until the spiders lose interest
Walk boldly to claim what you came for

stages.text: A forest spider drops from the highest thread and lands squarely in your path, eight eyes catching the fading silver light. It is the grove's guardian — large, deliberate, and absolutely unwilling to allow this kind of intrusion without a demonstration of seriousness.

choices.label: Fight the spider
Distract it with movement on the far side of the grove
Calm it with slow, deliberate movement
Find the gap in its web and slip through

stages.text: You are in the heart of the grove, the silk within reach. Harvesting it cleanly matters — crude cutting will leave the threads tangled and useless, and the fletcher will know. The spiders remain at the grove's edge. You have one careful opportunity.

choices.label: Use your blade to cut and gather quickly
Unwind the threads methodically from their anchor points
Harvest exactly what is needed, nothing more
Work out the anchor structure before touching a thread

stages.text: You have the silk — coiled, unbroken, warm in your palm even in the evening chill. The spiders withdraw as you back out of the grove slowly. The walk back to the fletcher feels lighter than the walk in, and you arrive just as the first proper dark settles over the trees.

choices.label: Present the silk to the fletcher directly
Inspect the silk under firelight before handing it over
Carry it back without disturbing it

stages.text: The fletcher runs the silk through her fingers once and pronounces it perfect without looking up. She works through the night and by morning the quiver is done — balanced, tight, better than anything in her shop.

quests.title: The Hermit's Last Prayer
quests.description: A dead hermit's shrine in the woods is drawing scavengers and restless spirits alike.

stages.text: A cairn of pale stones marks the hermit's resting place, ringed by fresh claw marks and scattered offerings that someone — or something — has rifled through. A pilgrim's staff leans against the largest stone, still bound with prayer strips that haven't faded. The woodcutters who reported the disturbance refuse to come within earshot after dark.

choices.label: Examine the claw marks on the cairn stones
Read the prayer strips on the staff carefully
Search the surrounding area for tracks
Set a camp nearby to watch through the day

stages.text: Just before dusk, a grave robber arrives and begins prying the cairn stones apart with a rusted bar, cursing quietly about the weight of the middle ones. She has knocked the prayer strips to the ground and is hunting for something she clearly believes is buried underneath. Her greed is making a cold blue light flicker inside the cairn with something that resembles fury.

choices.label: Confront her and demand she stop immediately
Fight the grave robber
Stomp on the prayer strips to protect them
Call out the hermit's name to interrupt her

stages.text: The grave robber is dealt with, but the cold light inside the cairn intensifies with moonrise. The temperature falls not gradually but all at once, the night sounds stop, and the blue glow resolves into something with presence and intent.

choices.label: Stand your ground and face what comes
Recite a prayer to prepare for the encounter
Study the light's pattern before it fully manifests
Back away and find cover to observe from

stages.text: A shadow wraith rises from the cairn's heart — tall and still, holding what appears to be a sealed letter pressed against its chest. It does not attack immediately. It watches you with hollow eyes, as though uncertain whether you are here to finally carry this burden or simply add to its weight.

choices.label: Fight the shadow wraith
Recite the prayer strips aloud from memory
Speak the hermit's name and state your purpose
Read the sealed letter the wraith is holding

stages.text: The letter crumbles to ash, the wraith bows once, and the cold light goes out. You are left alone with the cairn in the sudden quiet of the forest night. The hermit's staff is still where you found it, and when you lift it a small mace falls from the grip wrapping — long hidden, still balanced for a hand that no longer needs it.

choices.label: Rebuild the cairn stones properly before you go
Speak a final blessing over the grave
Take the mace and leave without ceremony

stages.text: The cairn grows still. The hermit's mace rests in your hand, sanctified by the rite you completed alone in the dark, with no one watching and no one to ask.

quests.title: Briarfoot Bandits
quests.description: Bandits have started laying snares in the brush to catch hunters and game alike.

stages.text: A snapped snare bites into a hunter's ankle and he is sitting on a log with a white face and a very specific look of someone who has had enough. 'They're not after coin anymore — they're taking whatever walks, man or beast.' He spreads the snare on the ground. It isn't crudely made. Someone who knows trapping put it here on purpose.

choices.label: Examine the snare's construction for identifying marks
Follow the snare line to find more of them
Ask the hunter where he found the others
Estimate from the snare how many bandits are operating

stages.text: The snare line leads deeper into the briarwood, each trap more carefully placed than the last. Someone has been walking these paths for weeks. At the third snare you find a half-eaten ration and a boot print that is too large for a goblin and too careless for a professional scout.

choices.label: Read the ration type and pack origin
Track the boot prints to their origin
Wait at the snare line to catch the next patrol
Mark each snare location to map the territory

stages.text: You spot the lookout perched in the gnarled oak above the main snare cluster — a bandit scout watching the approaches with a blade across his knees, eating something dried and completely bored. He hasn't spotted you yet.

choices.label: Silence the lookout quietly
Startle him into revealing himself
Find a better angle of approach before he turns
Circle wide and approach from the blind side

stages.text: Hidden amid the brambles, a bandit scout rises with a knife in hand and a whistle at his lips. He looks like someone who has done this many times and finds the whole thing very routine.

choices.label: Strike first before he can whistle
Knock the whistle away before engaging
Use the trigger mechanism of his own snare against him
Bluff that you have the camp surrounded

stages.text: The snares are cut and the scout is dealt with. You spend the better part of an hour pulling traps out of the ground and burning them in a pile, which feels deeply satisfying. The hunters will be able to walk these paths again.

choices.label: Cut and destroy every remaining snare you found
Mark the cleared area so hunters know it is safe
Report back with proof of what you found

stages.text: The hunters' leader rewards you with a set of dark leathers left by one of the bandits — well made, she admits, for people who turned out not to deserve them.

quests.title: Moonwell Vigil
quests.description: An old moonwell has begun whispering names of the dead into the water.

stages.text: A druid warns you not to drink from the moonwell under any circumstances, and she says it with the voice of someone who learned that lesson personally. 'The well remembers too much. Something below keeps calling souls back to it — the water sings names to anyone who leans in close, and three people have gone in since the rains.' The well is a mile north of the main path.

choices.label: Ask what the druid knows about the well's history
Ask the druid what the water said to her
Ask if there is a way to approach safely
Head for the moonwell immediately

stages.text: The moonwell sits in a clearing ringed by stones that are too evenly placed to be natural. The water inside is still and perfectly clear, and when you lean in slightly you can hear it — not voices exactly, but names, one after another, in a tone that makes you want to lean further. You do not.

choices.label: Study the ring stones for inscribed warnings
Walk the full ring to read the pattern
Find what has disturbed the well's seal
Look at the water without getting closer

stages.text: A grave acolyte emerges from the reeds at the well's far edge, carrying a water-warped prayerbook and a bone knife. It has been performing a rite at the moonwell for days, feeding the call that draws the living in. It notices you and raises the knife without hesitation.

choices.label: Fight the grave acolyte
Break the rite circle before it can complete the invocation
Shove the acolyte into its own trap
Counter-recite to interfere with the rite

stages.text: The acolyte is down but the water is still singing. The rite has been half-completed, and the moonwell's surface has begun to ripple from below as though something is rising to answer the call. The sigils around the ring are the key — if you can read and break them, you can silence the well.

choices.label: Break the sigil stones around the ring
Decipher and counter the active sigils
Pour the acolyte's prayerbook into the water
Recite the names back into the well to exhaust it

stages.text: The whispers fade into frogsong. The water clears, the names stop, and the moonwell settles back into an ordinary pool that reflects an ordinary sky. The druid is at the clearing's edge when you emerge, and she looks genuinely surprised that you are undrowned.

choices.label: Tell the druid exactly what you found and did
Help the druid mark the well as sealed
Accept the druid's offered thanks and leave

stages.text: The moonwell is quiet. The druid offers you a pocket scripture wrapped against damp and fog — the kind of thing you carry when you expect to need it and hope you don't.

quests.title: The White Feather Trail
quests.description: A wounded falcon keeps leading hunters toward the same bloodstained glade.

stages.text: Feathers as pale as frost drift between the trees in a way that feels deliberate rather than wind-blown. Somewhere ahead something thrashes in pain — a dry, repetitive sound that carries through the still air like an answer to a question nobody asked. The hunters who reported it say the falcon appears each morning and leads anyone who follows to the same glade.

choices.label: Examine a fallen feather for signs of injury
Follow the feather drift with the wind
Sense what direction the sound is coming from
Move toward the sound before it stops

stages.text: The glade is real. Churned earth and a metal glint identify the trap from twenty feet out — a poacher's cruelty wire, the kind designed to hold and not release. The falcon circles above with an injury on its right wing that it acquired trying to flag someone down, and the moss stag below it has been here long enough to have stopped fighting the wire.

choices.label: Read the trap type to understand how to open it
Approach the stag without startling it further
Keep the stag calm while you work the wire
Force the wire mechanism open by strength

stages.text: The poacher returns with bow drawn and a companion — he's been watching the approaches. 'Back off,' he says flatly. 'Stag's mine, bird's mine, clearing's mine.' His companion already has an arrow nocked and pointed at the falcon, not you.

choices.label: Draw steel on the poacher immediately
Rush the companion before the arrow is loosed
Talk the poacher down before blood is shed
Anticipate the companion's aim and dodge first

stages.text: The poacher's companion releases the arrow at the falcon — and the stag, suddenly free where you loosened the wire, throws itself between arrow and bird with all the grace of a creature that has made a decision. It takes the shot for the falcon and falls.

choices.label: Fight the poacher while the companion reloads
Tend the stag before the poachers can regroup
Drive the poachers off before they can finish it
Use the poacher's own wire as a weapon

stages.text: The poachers are gone and the stag lies still. The falcon lands beside it and does not move for a long moment. Then it looks at you with the amber clarity of something that intended to be seen, and places a talon on the ground where a silver-threaded charm has fallen from the stag's antler.

choices.label: Take the charm gently and step back
Give the clearing a proper moment before leaving
Mark the poacher's trails to prevent future visits

stages.text: The falcon circles once before vanishing into the canopy. Left behind is a talon charm tied with silver thread — the kind that catches light the way a falcon's eye does on a clear morning, when everything is possible and very few mistakes have been made yet.

quests.title: Tollhouse Embers
quests.description: A ruined tollhouse glows with banked coals each night though no keeper remains there.

stages.text: A merchant shivers despite the warm evening. 'No one sleeps near that tollhouse. The fire wakes after dark and so do the dead.' He points at a ruined stone building twenty yards off the road, cold in the afternoon light but already drawing sideways glances from every traveler. The keeper died three seasons ago, the post officially vacant.

choices.label: Examine the tollhouse in daylight for evidence
Ask travellers what they've witnessed at night
Scout the building from outside before entering
Wait and watch the tollhouse at dusk

stages.text: Inside the ruined tollhouse, the hearth holds a persistent warm glow that shouldn't be possible with no new fuel in months. The floor beneath the hearthstone has been disturbed recently — the mortar cracked not by age but by something working upward from below.

choices.label: Study the hearthstone and its cracked mortar
Lift the hearthstone by force
Listen at the cracks in the floor
Move quietly so whatever is below doesn't hear you

stages.text: Below the hearthstone is a shallow coin-cache, half-emptied by recent hands. A grave robber is crouched under the floor with a candle and a satchel, picking through old keeper's wages with the methodical efficiency of a professional. She freezes when she hears you.

choices.label: Confront her before she can flee with the coins
Fight the grave robber
Block her only exit quietly while she's distracted
Convince her that leaving the cache intact is in her interest

stages.text: The grave robber dealt with, the coals go cold as if the disruption was what fed them. But down in the cache you find something else — a small collection of personal effects belonging to the dead keeper, including a luck charm he apparently carried every day of his tenure and never got to pass on.

choices.label: Retrieve the charm carefully from the cache
Examine all the keeper's effects to understand him
Speak a brief acknowledgement over the spot
Reset the hearthstone and seal the cache

stages.text: The coals are cold and the tollhouse is quiet. The merchant who wouldn't sleep near the place is waiting outside with obvious questions, and you have satisfying answers for most of them.

choices.label: Explain what happened and what you found
Seal the tollhouse door before reporting back
Make sure the cache is truly secured

stages.text: The coals go cold and stay cold. Among the stolen trinkets you claim the keeper's charm — a small carved bone carried for luck on bad roads, still warm from thirty years of a good man's pockets.

quests.title: The Broken Standard
quests.description: An old battlefield banner keeps drawing revenants to a roadside shrine.

stages.text: Half-buried spearheads ring the shrine in a pattern too deliberate for chance. A torn battle-banner still flaps from the post though the wind is dead calm, and the merchants who use this road have learned to take the long way around after dark. 'It's the banner,' says the road-warden flatly. 'Everything comes back to the banner.'

choices.label: Examine the burial spears for identifying marks
Read the banner's heraldry to identify the unit
Ask the road-warden what she has witnessed here
Walk the shrine perimeter to understand its layout

stages.text: The shrine marks a field grave where an entire unit was buried together, their banner planted over them by whoever survived long enough to do it. The banner itself is bound with a soldier's oath-knot — a military tradition that ties the unit's spirit to the standard until someone releases it properly.

choices.label: Study the oath-knot binding to understand how to undo it
Try to pull the banner down by force
Look for any living soldiers who might remember the ritual
Walk the banner's edges to map the binding reach

stages.text: A pilgrim revenant rises from the earth beneath the banner at dusk — armored in the dried mud of a battlefield, carrying a broken spear, and moving with the purposeful clarity of something that knows exactly what it is waiting for. It looks at the banner before it looks at you.

choices.label: Fight the revenant
Cut the banner down first to disorient it
Name the unit's banner and speak its number
Stand between the revenant and the banner

stages.text: Two more revenants rise from adjacent burial mounds — this shrine holds more than one soldier, and the banner has been calling all of them. The oath-knot is still tied and the standard is still standing.

choices.label: Fight both revenants
Cut the oath-knot while they are distracted
Recite the military oath of release
Break the banner staff to release the binding

stages.text: With the banner released or the revenants dispersed, the shrine grows quiet in a way that feels earned rather than empty. The road-warden walks up behind you and nods once. Among the relics near the base of the post is a tower heater shield, still sound, carried here by one of the buried and never claimed.

choices.label: Take the shield with the road-warden's blessing
Mark the shrine properly for travelers to avoid
Seal the burial mounds to prevent further risings

stages.text: The shrine is quiet. A weathered tower heater shield leans against the post, still sturdy after everything — claimed now by the person who finished what a battlefield left unfinished.

quests.title: Caravan of Knives
quests.description: A caravan master believes his own guards are selling travelers to roadside killers.

stages.text: The caravan master speaks low, with the flat affect of someone who has been betrayed before and is trying very hard not to show how much it costs him. 'Someone tips the bandits off before we make camp. The attacks are too precise — someone knows our route, our schedule, our cargo.' He looks at the guards around the fire. 'Tonight I want the truth.'

choices.label: Ask the master which guards he suspects most
Study each guard's behaviour during camp setup
Offer to watch from inside the cargo instead
Volunteer to run your own patrol route

stages.text: From your position among the cargo you can see the whole camp clearly. The guards do their rounds in pairs until around midnight, when one of them develops a reason to walk the far end of the wagon line alone, moving with the careful casualness of someone doing something they've rehearsed.

choices.label: Follow the guard from a safe distance
Read the guard's route for the handoff location
Alert the master quietly before moving
Move to cut off the handoff point before he reaches it

stages.text: Near midnight, a highway bandit approaches the wagon from the treeline to collect payment from inside the camp — a small bag, maps, and what appears to be a schedule for the next three stops. The transaction is nearly complete.

choices.label: Ambush the bandit contact
Intercept the payment bag before it changes hands
Record what you see and present it to the master first
Confront both traitor and bandit simultaneously

stages.text: The contact is dealt with but the traitor guard has bolted. He has a head start and a horse and clearly had an escape planned. The master is awake now and watching everything with the expression of a man who hoped he was wrong.

choices.label: Chase the traitor down on foot
Predict where he'll go and cut him off
Use the recovered maps to identify his bolt-hole
Rally the other guards to give chase

stages.text: The traitor is brought back. The master listens to everything without expression, then dismisses the guard and presses the caravan's most expensive cargo against your hands — a set of shadow-forged daggers he was transporting for a buyer who, he says, can wait.

choices.label: Accept the daggers and the master's thanks
Help the master secure the rest of the route
Make sure the remaining guards are trustworthy

stages.text: The traitors are exposed. The grateful master rewards you with a pair of balanced shadow-forged daggers, and offers the kind of careful handshake that means he expects to ask for your help again someday.

quests.title: Escort to the Chapel Road
quests.description: A novice priest needs safe passage to a ruined roadside chapel where survivors have gathered.

stages.text: The novice clutches a lantern and a prayer in either hand and looks like someone who has walked a long way on not enough sleep. 'If I do not reach them by dawn they will lose hope before healing.' The chapel is four miles down a road with three bad stretches and no company until morning. He looks at you with the quiet directness of someone who is not actually asking you to choose.

choices.label: Ask the novice what dangers he has heard of on the road
Study the road ahead for the worst stretch
Agree to escort and start walking
Convince the novice to rest first and leave at first light

stages.text: Two miles in, the road narrows at a broken ford where the stone has heaved up and the water runs black at night. The novice says this is where the last escort turned back. The lantern catches a pair of eyes in the grass on the bank that is not a reflection.

choices.label: Step forward and draw the attention away from the novice
Cross quickly and quietly before it reacts
Identify what is watching from its movement pattern
Take the high bank and force it back into the water

stages.text: A road serpent strikes from the broken milestone at the ford's edge, drawn by the lantern's warmth and the smell of a frightened young man who clearly has never been this far from a city after dark.

choices.label: Fight the road serpent
Drive it off with the lantern flame
Pull the novice aside and let the serpent over-extend
Smash the milestone it shelters beneath

stages.text: The serpent is past but the novice is shaking and the lantern is flickering after the struggle. There are still two miles to go and one bad stretch remaining — a collapsed section of road where the ground drops away on the left and the tree line comes in close on the right.

choices.label: Walk the collapsed edge carefully
Scout ahead before letting the novice follow
Carry the lantern and take point
Keep the novice talking to maintain his courage

stages.text: The chapel appears at the end of a straight stretch of road just as the first grey light appears behind the eastern hills. Survivors are visible at the windows. The novice breaks into a run the moment he sees the torchlight inside, and the chapel door opens before he reaches it.

choices.label: Follow the novice in and ensure the survivors are safe
Stand watch outside while the novice does his work
Help settle the survivors when you enter

stages.text: The chapel survivors are saved. The priest thanks you with a sun charm blessed against fear and fatigue — the kind of thing you carry not because you believe in it, he says, but because sometimes you do.

quests.title: The Blackwheel Chase
quests.description: A stolen courier wagon has vanished toward the Crossroads carrying sealed maps and scout pay.

stages.text: You find black wheel tracks carved deep into the mud, straight and hard and heading fast for the next fork in the road. The courier post's sergeant is behind you with a look of professional fury. 'Those are scout supply maps. If the wrong hands get them, three outposts go dark before spring.' She hands you the warrant. 'Ride them down.'

choices.label: Read the tracks to estimate the wagon's speed and load
Predict which fork the thieves will take
Run the tracks down before they get further ahead
Cut cross-country to get ahead of the wagon

stages.text: The wheel tracks lead into the bandit country east of the road before cutting sharply south — whoever took the wagon knows the land. You find where they stopped: a camp ring with embers still warm, maps spread on a rock, and three sets of boot prints heading in different directions. They've split up.

choices.label: Read the spread maps for what was already copied
Track the prints to determine which one has the originals
Follow the freshest set of tracks
Recover the maps left on the rock immediately

stages.text: The wagon lies overturned at the base of a short embankment, the axle snapped. Someone was driving too fast for the road and paid for it. A masked cutpurse scrambles from the ditch beside it, clutching a leather satchel to her chest with the determination of someone whose entire plan now depends on her legs.

choices.label: Run her down before she reaches the treeline
Cut her off by taking the high bank
Predict where she'll run and intercept her
Shout the warrant threat to make her stop

stages.text: The cutpurse is cornered at the treeline with the satchel still in hand. She looks at the warrant, then at you, then back at the warrant. 'Most people take the bribe,' she says. 'What do you want for these?'

choices.label: Fight the masked cutpurse
Refuse the bribe and take the satchel by force
Trick her into revealing where her contact is
Disarm her and demand the satchel

stages.text: The maps are recovered. The cutpurse is in custody and the satchel is in your hands. The scout sergeant arrives twenty minutes later on a borrowed horse and goes through the maps with a professional silence that means everything is there.

choices.label: Hand over the maps and give a full account
Help the sergeant secure the prisoner for transport
Accept the reward and the sergeant's respect

stages.text: The maps are recovered. The scouts let you keep their weatherproof mantle as payment — standard issue, which is a compliment in its own way.

quests.title: The Queen's Mile
quests.description: A legendary stretch of the King's Road still remembers the soldiers who died defending it.

stages.text: Milestones older than the kingdom line this stretch of road, each one carved with a name scratched by hands that no longer exist in any record. The road-keeper's notes say something woke here after the last storm — not hostile, exactly, but testing. 'Walk it in silence,' she says, 'and it will let you pass. Walk it wrong and it won't.'

choices.label: Read the carved names on the milestones
Walk the mile slowly and without drawing a weapon
Ask the road-keeper what walking it wrong looks like
Walk it at pace and see what happens

stages.text: The stones grow denser near the road's midpoint — not just milestones now but grave markers, hundreds of them barely visible in the grass on either side. The carved names continue. Someone spent a very long time recording the dead of this road, and the road has not forgotten.

choices.label: Trace the names from oldest to newest to understand the battle
Walk between the markers without touching them
Speak aloud the names you pass to acknowledge them
Keep walking steadily without slowing

stages.text: At the crest of the hill, a deserter lancer waits among a collection of rusted helms laid at the roadside. He doesn't look like a ghost — he looks like a man who came back and found there was nothing left to come back to. He is testing whether you are worth the history of this road.

choices.label: Speak the names carved on the nearest milestone
Stand your ground without explaining yourself
Challenge the lancer's claim on the road
Identify his regiment from his rusted insignia

stages.text: The lancer raises his weapon — the test wasn't rhetorical. He attacks with the controlled precision of someone who has done this many times, each strike designed not to kill but to find the measure of whoever is walking this road.

choices.label: Fight the deserter lancer
Fight defensively until he exhausts his test
Endure the strikes and refuse to yield
Match his precision with your own

stages.text: The lancer stops. He looks at you for a long moment, then at the road, then kneels once in the direction of the oldest stone. When he rises he is already dissolving into the light of midday. Buried beneath the last milestone is a shirt of vanguard mail — heavy, clean, and clearly waiting.

choices.label: Dig up the cache beneath the last milestone
Acknowledge the road and its dead before taking it
Walk the last stretch to the end before claiming anything

stages.text: The old mile accepts your passing. Buried beneath the last stone is a shirt of vanguard mail — and something else: a soldier's coin, pressed flat, that traveled with someone the whole length of the road and never made it home.

quests.title: The Forgewarning
quests.description: A shrine in Ironhold's outer forge keeps ringing though no hammer touches it.

stages.text: A forgewarden listens to the bell with the expression of someone who has been sleeping badly for a week and would like the situation resolved before another one begins. 'Each ring means something crawled out of the lower tunnels — at least that's what the old hands say. Three rings yesterday. Five this morning. Something is coming up.' The shrine bell hangs in the smelting antechamber, connected to nothing visible.

choices.label: Examine the bell mechanism for a physical trigger
Ask the old hands what the bell has predicted before
Inspect the lower tunnel access points near the shrine
Stand watch at the shrine until something rings it

stages.text: The bell rings again while you're watching — not from any mechanism but from the air itself, a sympathetic resonance triggered by movement in the tunnels below. You can feel the vibration through the soles of your boots, rhythmic and ascending. Whatever is coming up is close.

choices.label: Identify which tunnel the vibration is rising from
Calculate the rate of ascent from the bell frequency
Get below the bell line before it arrives at this level
Alert the forge crew and clear the antechamber

stages.text: In the upper access tunnel, you find scorched prayer plates half-molten and shattered where something moved through them. Fire beetles have been feeding on the heat residue and are now clustered at the shrine antechamber entrance, drawn by the warmth of the active forge. There are more than you can count at a glance.

choices.label: Drive the beetle cluster away from the entrance
Seal the entrance they're using before more arrive
Use the forge heat to redirect them back downward
Move the powder casks clear before engaging

stages.text: Below the forge, one fire beetle much larger than the others skitters over the half-molten prayer plates and orients toward the powder casks at the tunnel junction. It has been feeding here long enough to grow and is now large enough to be genuinely dangerous.

choices.label: Fight the fire beetle
Kick the powder casks clear before it reaches them
Douse the heat trail it's following with water
Use the forge flue to exhaust the heat it needs

stages.text: The beetle is dealt with and the bell stops. The forgewardens seal the lower access behind you with iron plates while you stand catching your breath in the smelting antechamber. The shrine bell hangs still and silent, which everyone agrees is the correct state for it.

choices.label: Help seal and reinforce the lower access tunnels
Advise the forgewardens on early warning signs to watch for
Accept the forgewardens' thanks and debrief

stages.text: The forgewardens praise your speed and gift you a sanctified mace kept for tunnel ward duty — a tool of the forge, they say, for someone who treated the job like one.

quests.title: Tunnels of Slag
quests.description: Miners report hearing arrows rattling somewhere deep below where no archers should be.

stages.text: A soot-faced miner whispers like someone who suspects the walls can hear. 'Something is stealing our fletching and leaving tracks in the slag. Not human tracks. Something bigger, heavier, with knees that don't bend the way they should.' He marks three galleries on the map. 'Started a week ago. Now it's every shift.'

choices.label: Examine the tracks in the slag for species and size
Follow the pattern of missing fletching between galleries
Go down to the affected galleries directly
Ask which miners have seen the most and interview them

stages.text: The tracks are hobgoblin — heavy, deliberate, with the spacing of something that knows where it is going and doesn't bother hiding. The fletching theft pattern shows a clear progression: whoever this is started at the furthest gallery and has been working systematically inward. They're building something.

choices.label: Predict where they're assembling the stolen materials
Follow the track line toward the deepest gallery
Identify what they're building from the theft pattern
Move through the collapsed section to get ahead of them

stages.text: In a collapsed gallery you find the stash: scavenged bows, mine rope, ore sacks, and a crude fortification of timber props arranged into something that looks like a defensive position. This wasn't random opportunism — someone planned to hold this section. And the planning is not quite finished.

choices.label: Dismantle the fortification before the occupant returns
Study the fortification's intended function
Wait at the fortification's blind side for the hobgoblin
Work out what it would take to hold this gallery yourself

stages.text: The hobgoblin bruiser returns and does not seem surprised by your presence — it had a secondary entry, apparently, and has been aware of you since the second gallery. It drops two ore sacks and picks up a weapon with the settled expression of someone who was going to have to do this eventually.

choices.label: Fight the hobgoblin bruiser
Drop the loose beam above it before it can engage
Use the fortification's own layout against it
Overwhelm it with direct force before it can set

stages.text: The hobgoblin is down. The miners reclaim their tools and the stolen materials with a speed that suggests they've been waiting nearby, which they have. The foreman offers the gallery back to regular use and a promise to properly seal the external access the hobgoblin was using.

choices.label: Help dismantle the crude fortification for salvage
Map the external access route so it can be sealed
Advise the foreman on watch procedures going forward

stages.text: The miners reclaim their tools and repay you with a yew longbow from the fortress stores — well-kept, powerful, recovered from a previous security incident and never claimed by anyone with a better right to it.

quests.title: Ledger of Ash
quests.description: A bookkeeper's records keep vanishing whenever ore goes missing from the lower docks.

stages.text: The quartermaster taps a burnt ledger on the table between you. 'Someone wants these numbers forgotten. Three months of ore weight records, gone. I know the tonnage that left this dock and I know the tonnage that arrived at the smelter, and the difference has a name I cannot prove.' He slides you a pass for the lower docks. 'Find the hand behind it.'

choices.label: Examine the burnt ledger for recoverable data
Study the dock access logs for unauthorized entries
Stake out the dock during the night shift
Ask the dock workers what they've noticed changing

stages.text: The access log shows one person's credentials being used at off-hours three weeks running — the same shift, the same tunnel, the same fifteen-minute window. Someone is careful but not quite careful enough. The credentials belong to a clerk who, according to everyone you ask, has been spending significantly above her station.

choices.label: Identify where the off-hours access leads in the tunnels
Follow the access pattern to find the handoff point
Get into position at the handoff tunnel before the next window
Confront the clerk directly to read her reaction

stages.text: You reach the handoff point — a side gallery off the lower dock, dark and rarely used. A rogue mercenary is already there waiting, and he has the current version of the ledger under his arm. He's early. You're earlier.

choices.label: Cut him off before he can secure the ledger
Confront him with the quartermaster's authority
Block both exits before engaging
Rush him before he can assess the situation

stages.text: A rogue mercenary slips from the shadow of a storage crane with a satchel full of receipts and stolen seals. He moves like someone who was expecting a different kind of welcome and is now performing a fast recalculation.

choices.label: Fight the rogue mercenary
Switch the satchel before he notices the approach
Trap him between the crane and the gallery wall
Break his nerve with immediate aggressive action

stages.text: The records are recovered, the mercenary is in custody, and the clerk's name is in the receipts in the satchel. The quartermaster receives everything with the particular silence of someone who was right about the amount and wrong about the person.

choices.label: Deliver the full evidence chain to the quartermaster
Help organise the recovered records for the inquiry
Accept the quartermaster's formal thanks

stages.text: The records are saved. The quartermaster awards you a signet once used by a famous smuggler-turned-scout — recovered years ago from a different operation and kept for someone who understood how the game worked.

quests.title: The Stone Psalm
quests.description: Old dwarven prayers echo from a blocked shaft where the dead were never properly sealed.

stages.text: A greybeard priest lays his hand on the mine wall with the careful posture of someone listening through stone. 'Hear that? The stone remembers a funeral left unfinished — a whole crew sealed in when the shaft collapsed, still with their tools, still mid-prayer.' He removes his hand. 'It's been a hundred years. The stone is getting louder.'

choices.label: Listen to the wall yourself and try to understand the sound
Ask the priest what the prayers are saying
Examine the blocked shaft entrance for safe access
Ask which miners have heard it and when it started getting louder

stages.text: The shaft is accessible through a secondary tunnel that collapsed partially rather than fully. The stone around the entrance shows hairline cracks where something on the other side has been pressing outward — not forcefully, but persistently, for a very long time.

choices.label: Clear the secondary tunnel entrance carefully
Read the crack patterns for structural safety
Slip through the gap that already exists
Listen at the cracks before committing to entry

stages.text: Within the sealed shaft, the air is perfectly still and smells of old stone and something that hasn't been air for a hundred years. The mining crew's equipment is exactly where they left it. The prayer marks are carved into every surface. And at the far end of the shaft, a shadow wraith circles a reliquary lantern, feeding on the unfinished grief trapped in this chamber.

choices.label: Approach the wraith to assess whether it is aware of you
Read the prayer marks to understand what the crew was saying
Study the reliquary lantern for its significance
Announce yourself clearly so the wraith knows your intent

stages.text: The shadow wraith turns its full attention to you — it has been here a hundred years waiting for someone to either finish the prayer or take the lantern, and it is no longer patient enough to wait for the distinction.

choices.label: Fight the shadow wraith
Finish the funeral rite from the carved marks
Take the reliquary lantern and carry it out into the light
Speak the names carved into the tools and equipment

stages.text: The stone falls quiet. The wraith dissolves and the prayer marks stop resonating. The priest is at the shaft entrance when you emerge, looking at the stone wall with the expression of someone listening to a silence they have waited years to hear.

choices.label: Give the priest the full account of what you found inside
Help seal the shaft entrance properly now that it can rest
Present the reliquary to the priest for formal keeping

stages.text: The stone falls quiet. The priest places the recovered reliquary in your keeping for brave service — a century-old lantern, still lit, still warm, as though it never stopped waiting for someone to carry it.

quests.title: Breaker of Chains
quests.description: Slave collars from an old war have surfaced in the mines, and someone is trying to sell them as relics.

stages.text: A veteran miner spits on the floor and doesn't apologize for it. 'Those belong in a furnace, not a market — someone has been selling them to collectors in Sunspire as war antiques.' He sets one on the table. The locking mechanism still works. 'Find who is trafficking them. Whatever it costs.'

choices.label: Examine the collar's markings for manufacture origin
Ask the veteran which miners have been keeping unusual hours
Follow the money trail backward from the Sunspire sales
Search the lower galleries for a storage cache

stages.text: The trail leads down. Someone has been using a side chamber in the restricted lower section as a staging area — collars in, coin out, no paperwork. The miners on the level above have been bribed to look the other way. The buyers are private collectors, and the source is inside the mine.

choices.label: Identify which miners took the bribes and interview them
Trace the restricted access records for unauthorized entries
Find the side chamber before the next shipment is moved
Get into the restricted section without authorization

stages.text: You find the side chamber, and it is larger than expected — not a stopgap cache but a proper operation, with shelving, packing materials, and what appears to be a ledger. Someone has been running this for years. The guardian of the chamber is very large and very old and still completely functional.

choices.label: Study the golem's construction for weaknesses
Attempt to find and access the golem's control mechanism
Draw the golem away from the ledger
Engage the golem directly and push through it

stages.text: In a chamber beyond the cache, a mine golem guards a hoard of collars, coin, and one noble blade left in tribute. It moves with the slow certainty of something that was wound up for a specific purpose and has not yet been told that purpose is over.

choices.label: Fight the mine golem
Shatter the support crystal above it to bring it down
Find the golem's deactivation point in the chamber
Overpower the golem before it can orient fully

stages.text: The golem falls. The collars are recovered, the ledger is seized, and the chamber is collapsed behind you. The veteran miner and two others are already building a smelter fire when you arrive with the collars. They don't say much. They don't need to.

choices.label: Watch the collars go into the furnace yourself
Deliver the ledger to the mine authority for prosecution
Accept the veteran's thanks on behalf of those the collars held

stages.text: The collars are destroyed. The veteran leaves the recovered knightly sword in your care — from the chamber, he says, and it should go to someone who burned something that deserved burning.

quests.title: Smoke Below Ironhold
quests.description: A poison haze is creeping up from disused vents beneath the fortress taverns.

stages.text: The tavern keeper coughs into her sleeve and has been doing so for three days. 'That smoke tastes wrong — too bitter for forge soot, too sweet for fire damage, and it's getting stronger after midnight.' Two of her staff haven't come to work. She doesn't want to say what she suspects. 'It's coming up from the vents.'

choices.label: Identify the smoke composition from smell and residue
Map the vent system to find the source level
Descend into the vent access to investigate directly
Check on the missing staff first to assess exposure severity

stages.text: The smoke is alchemical — not industrial but deliberate, the kind produced by specific combinations of compounds that someone with a working knowledge of chemistry chose on purpose. The vent system carries it upward, which means someone below knows where the vents lead. This isn't accidental.

choices.label: Trace the compound type to likely sources in the lower city
Find the vent source before the fumes concentrate further
Read the vent pattern to identify who has access
Seal the upper vent connections to protect the taverns while you work

stages.text: You find shattered poison flasks simmering in the heat of the lower tunnels around a slag hound that has been feeding on the chemical warmth and is now very large and not at all calm. The flasks are still actively venting. The hound guards the area around them with the territorial certainty of something that considers this its home.

choices.label: Fight the slag hound
Use the vents to smother the hound's heat source
Move through the tunnel while the hound is distracted by the fumes
Draw it away from the flask cluster before engaging

stages.text: The hound is dealt with, but the shattered flasks are still venting. Someone set up a deliberate slow-release mechanism — the flasks were placed in a heat-exposure pattern to produce maximum output over maximum time. This was designed to last weeks. Stopping it requires dismantling each flask safely.

choices.label: Dismantle each flask carefully using known alchemical counter-agents
Smash the heat source feeding the venting to stop the reaction
Seal the vent junctions to contain the fumes below the taverns
Remove each flask from the heat source without triggering them

stages.text: The fumes clear by the following morning. The tavern keeper's cough improves within hours. You find a name scratched into the corner of one of the flask casings — the same name that appears in the ledger recovered from the collar operation. Someone in Ironhold has been busy.

choices.label: Report the name to the mine authority with the full evidence
Follow the lead further before reporting
Let the tavern keeper know the source of the smoke

stages.text: The fumes clear and the keeper rewards you with a satchel of discreet alchemical tools she would rather not keep near her cellar — gathered, she says, from the last person who caused her this kind of trouble.

quests.title: Candles at Noon
quests.description: Someone has been leaving lit funeral candles at the crossroads in broad daylight.

stages.text: A peddler lowers her voice even though the crossroads is busy and bright. 'Every candle burns down to nothing in moments, and then someone goes missing by dusk. Three travelers since the new moon.' The candles are arranged in a pattern she can't explain — not random, not decorative. Something is marking them.

choices.label: Study the candle arrangement pattern
Ask witnesses to describe what happens when they burn
Read the wax for origin and quality
Wait beside the candles while people still avoid them

stages.text: You identify the pattern — the candles mark the exact spots where the missing travelers were last seen. Each marking is deliberate, placed precisely. Something is watching the crossroads and choosing its targets, and the candles are not cause but symptom.

choices.label: Follow the sight lines from each candle to find the watcher
Map the candle positions to identify the central point
Move between the markers to draw attention
Announce loudly that you know what the pattern means

stages.text: A will-o'-wisp blooms out of the waxlight at noon — not at night, in full daylight, which means it has grown confident enough to stop hiding. It drifts toward the hanging tree at the crossroads edge, pulsing with a cold invitation that targets anyone who is frightened or uncertain or simply too tired to think clearly.

choices.label: Chase the wisp down before it reaches the tree
Trap it inside a lantern before it can flee
Focus and resist its pull while closing the gap
Shatter the nearest candle to disrupt its anchor

stages.text: The will-o'-wisp has reached the hanging tree and is circling it with the patience of something that has been doing this for weeks. Three more wisps rise from the ground around the tree's roots — this is not a lone spirit but a colony, and the tree is where they feed.

choices.label: Fight the will-o'-wisp
Sever the wisps from their anchor at the tree roots
Drive them away from the tree with fire
Contain them in sequence before they can combine

stages.text: The noon haunt is broken. The candles stop appearing and the crossroads feels like itself again — loud, busy, and completely uninterested in the supernatural. A cleric who has been trying to deal with this for two weeks finds you at the inn that evening with an offering.

choices.label: Accept the cleric's thanks and explanation
Help the cleric mark where the wisps were anchored
Tell the cleric what you found so she can prevent recurrence

stages.text: The noon haunt is broken. A thankful cleric places prayer beads in your hand before resuming the road — the kind blessed specifically against disorientation, which she says she suspects you'll find useful.

quests.title: The Crow Feather Contract
quests.description: A courier never arrived after taking a sealed contract through bandit country.

stages.text: Black feathers mark the beginning of the courier trail — placed deliberately, one per quarter-mile, as if someone wanted to be followed or mocked. The courier guild agent is past worried and into the cold professional mode of someone calculating losses. 'The contract was worth more than the courier's pay. Someone knew what was in the satchel.'

choices.label: Examine the black feathers for origin
Read the wagon ruts for direction and speed
Ask the guild agent what the contract contained
Follow the feather trail immediately

stages.text: The trail leads to a hollow where the courier's horse is tied and unbothered. The courier himself is not here — but the satchel is, hidden under a root with the seal intact. Someone robbed the courier but left the contract. Either they didn't know what they had, or they were interrupted.

choices.label: Read the area for signs of what interrupted the robbery
Examine the satchel and its hidden position
Track the courier from his last position
Follow the newer set of footprints leaving the hollow

stages.text: The courier is ahead, being escorted at knife-point by a highway bandit also looking for the satchel. The bandit is focused on the courier and neither has noticed you yet. This window is narrow.

choices.label: Create a diversion and pull the courier free
Announce yourself to draw the bandit's attention
Assess the bandit's vulnerabilities before committing
Bluff that you already have the satchel

stages.text: A highway bandit waits by the courier's decoy satchel, convinced no one will challenge him this far off the main road. He turns when you arrive and recalibrates quickly.

choices.label: Fight the highway bandit
Disarm him before he can commit to the attack
Invoke courier authority to wrong-foot him
Overpower him with superior positioning

stages.text: The bandit is down, the courier is free, and the actual contract is still under the root where the robber missed it. The courier retrieves it with shaking hands and holds it to his chest for a long moment before looking up.

choices.label: Escort the courier back to the crossroads
Collect the bandit's information for the guild agent
Accept the courier's sworn thanks

stages.text: The contract is recovered. The courier's guild pays you with a reinforced quiver prized by outriders — the kind of thing that survives a hard road and still works when you need it.

quests.title: Gambler's Grave
quests.description: A card sharp was buried at the crossroads with his winnings, and now his debtors keep vanishing.

stages.text: Coins glitter in the dirt around a shallow grave marker, but none of them stay where they are left — travelers report placing them out of respect and finding them rearranged each morning into a hand of cards. The gambler buried here owed debts and was owed them in equal measure, and apparently neither side of that ledger has closed.

choices.label: Read the marker for the gambler's name and dates
Study the coin arrangement for a pattern
Ask at the crossroads inn who the gambler was
Dig at the grave to find what is actually there

stages.text: The gambler's history comes together: a professional cheat who won too much from the wrong people, was buried here by his creditors, and apparently never told anyone where the winnings were hidden. Three of those creditors have since vanished after visiting the grave late at night.

choices.label: Stake out the grave during the dangerous hours
Research the disappeared creditors for a pattern
Read the grave site for physical evidence of the disappearances
Make it known you are there to settle the debt

stages.text: Coins begin moving on their own near midnight — sliding across the dirt in patterns too deliberate for wind. The gambler's luck, whatever it was, still runs here. And a masked cutpurse is watching from behind the marker stone, waiting for someone to be distracted enough to rob.

choices.label: Spot the cutpurse and call them out
Let the cutpurse make their move first
Circle around the marker before they realise you've seen them
Challenge them to identify themselves

stages.text: The masked cutpurse springs from behind the marker with a knife already in hand — no negotiation, no preamble, just a professional assessment that you are between her and the game she's been running here all month.

choices.label: Fight the masked cutpurse
Disarm her before she can use the knife effectively
Use the grave's uneven ground to your advantage
Pin her with overwhelming force

stages.text: The cutpurse is dealt with. The coins settle into a final arrangement — a full hand, face up, laid out as though someone wanted it seen. Below the marker stone is the gambler's actual stash, and in it a fine nightcloak that apparently never went into the ground with its owner.

choices.label: Consecrate the grave properly now that the debt is settled
Take the nightcloak and the remaining coins
Report the cutpurse's operation to the crossroads warden

stages.text: The grave is consecrated and the gambler's luck finally spent. Among the effects is a fine nightcloak — the gambler's best piece, it turns out, worn on the nights when the stakes were real.

quests.title: Rope and Sigil
quests.description: A hanged templar's body disappeared years ago, but his sigils still flare on the rope in storms.

stages.text: Rain-dark bark creaks over the road in the still air between storms. The old sigils carved into the hanging tree's bark spark with pale light when wet, cycling through three symbols that a cleric identified as a templar's personal oath-seal. 'He was hanged here,' she says, 'but the body vanished before it could be brought down. That's the problem.'

choices.label: Study the sigil cycle for its meaning and sequence
Ask the cleric everything she knows about the templar
Stay through the storm to observe the full cycle
Ask if anyone witnessed the execution and the disappearance

stages.text: You piece together the templar's history: executed for a crime he did not commit, hanged on false testimony, his body removed by the order that wronged him to prevent martyrdom. The sigils are his oath — to seek acknowledgement and to not rest until it is given. The oath has been running for forty years.

choices.label: Research the names of those who gave false testimony
Find the templar's original records to reconstruct the truth
Mark the full extent of the oath-binding at the tree
Ask the cleric order to officially acknowledge the injustice

stages.text: Keep vigil through the storm. The sigils flare with the lightning and the rope sheds water like something alive, swinging in a windless rain. A shadow forms beneath the tree's worst branch — not yet fully manifest, but gathering.

choices.label: Hold your ground while the manifestation builds
Prepare a warding to contain what is coming
Study the manifestation pattern to understand its limits
Speak to the forming spirit before it fully sets

stages.text: A pilgrim revenant forms beneath the rope — the templar's enduring presence, armored in the image of what he wore on the day of his execution, still carrying the oath in every line of his posture. He looks at the sigils, then at you, and waits.

choices.label: Fight the pilgrim revenant
Speak the names of the guilty aloud at the site
Read aloud the templar's original oath from the records
Cut down the rope to release the oath-binding

stages.text: The revenant bows and dissolves. Folded at the base of the tree, preserved despite forty years of weather by the oath that kept them here, are templar vestments — the ones he was buried in, apparently, since the body was never found.

choices.label: Retrieve the vestments and bring them to the cleric order
Mark the site so the templar's name can be properly recorded
Take the vestments as the oath's resolution

stages.text: The revenant bows and dissolves. Folded beside the tree roots are preserved templar vestments — forty years old and intact, waiting for someone who finished what a false verdict left undone.

quests.title: Trial of the Roadwarden
quests.description: A retired roadwarden offers to test whether you can truly hold the roads together.

stages.text: The old warden plants a spear in the earth and looks at you the way a craftsman looks at a tool they're not certain will hold. 'Anyone can kill. I've seen a thousand people who can kill. What I have not seen in ten years is someone who can keep a road safe while people are still using it.' He gestures at the crossroads. 'Prove the difference.'

choices.label: Ask the warden what the trial involves specifically
Study the crossroads for existing vulnerabilities
Accept the trial immediately without clarifying terms
Ask what previous people who took this trial did wrong

stages.text: The warden outlines the trial: you must keep traffic moving, threats dealt with, and no traveler harmed for a full day's watch. The crossroads is busy and the threats are real — they've been escalating this past week specifically because word got out that the warden was testing candidates.

choices.label: Set up patrol routes to cover the crossroads approaches
Read the incoming travelers for immediate threat levels
Position yourself at the highest-risk approach
Coordinate with local merchants to share watch duties

stages.text: A road serpent has moved to the ford just south of the crossroads — not lethal but unpredictable, and the merchants crossing with heavy wagons can't afford a spooked horse. You have to deal with it without stopping traffic or creating a scene that draws more attention than the serpent itself.

choices.label: Drive the serpent off with fire from the roadside
Remove it by hand while the cart traffic pauses briefly
Lure it off the ford without traffic noticing
Alert only the drivers who need to know and not the crowds

stages.text: A rogue mercenary and his gang of hired roughs move to rob a pilgrim camp on the crossroads edge right on schedule — as though they knew when the regular patrol would be elsewhere. They are organized, experienced, and currently walking toward vulnerable people.

choices.label: Fight the rogue mercenary
Rally the pilgrims to defend themselves first
Flank the gang while their leader focuses forward
Break the gang's formation before they can commit

stages.text: The gang is dispersed. The pilgrims are unhurt. The warden has been watching the whole day from different positions — you spotted him twice, which he says is an improvement on most. He walks over and says nothing for a long moment, which is apparently how he says well done.

choices.label: Give the warden a full account of what you observed today
Report the gang's likely origin for follow-up
Accept the warden's assessment without pressing him for more

stages.text: The camp stands unbroken. The warden knots an old war banner charm around your wrist and calls you worthy — in the same tone he would use to describe a bridge that held under maximum load, which from him is high praise.

quests.title: Lantern Run
quests.description: A smuggler's runner moves through the marsh road each dusk, guided by shuttered lantern signals.

stages.text: You crouch in the reeds and count lantern blinks between milestones and dead trees — one long, two short, pause — repeated from three different positions that shift each night. A smuggling signal system running on a schedule. Whoever set it up knows the roads and knows who patrols them, which means they have a source inside the crossroads post.

choices.label: Decode the signal pattern and map the relay positions
Track where each signal originates from
Move through the reeds without breaking cover
Scout the relay positions before dark

stages.text: The relay system has three stations: two fixed and one moving. The moving one is the runner — the person who actually carries the goods and the information. If you can intercept the runner, you cut the whole operation in one action rather than chasing down every individual relay.

choices.label: Identify the runner's route from the signal timing
Predict the runner's handoff location
Position yourself ahead of the runner's path
Disable one relay to force the runner to improvise

stages.text: A dire rat swarm bursts from the culvert the runner uses as a shelter stop — not wild, but bait placed deliberately to slow anyone who follows. The rats are between you and the runner's next position. Whoever set this up anticipated pursuit.

choices.label: Fight the dire rat swarm
Smoke the culvert to drive them out
Go around the culvert via the high bank
Charge through before they can swarm properly

stages.text: The rats are past but the runner knows you are here — a lantern blinks twice fast from further down the road, a warning signal. The runner is already moving away from the scheduled route, using the backup paths that only someone with real knowledge of this road would know.

choices.label: Sprint to cut off the backup route exit
Use the signal system to send false directions
Predict where the backup path exits and wait there
Push through the marsh directly to intercept

stages.text: The runner is cornered at the route's dead end with the goods satchel and a look that combines professional respect with genuine disappointment. The satchel contains not just contraband but a list of crossroads post informants — the inside source the smugglers have been using for months.

choices.label: Confiscate everything and deliver it to the post warden
Interrogate the runner for the full network structure
Use the informant list as leverage for a larger operation

stages.text: The route is exposed and the runner's trick satchel becomes yours when the smugglers scatter. The warden gives you the contents of the runner's own supply bag — including a smoke pouch the runner kept for exactly the situation you just put them in.

quests.title: Bogside Mass
quests.description: A drowned chapel bell tolls beneath the swamp each dawn, and the dead answer in procession.

stages.text: At first light, shapes move in the mist toward a sunken chapel door just below the waterline — not random, not erratic, but in procession, each one in the same gap between the next. A ferryman on the far bank has been watching this happen every morning for two weeks and has stopped sleeping in his boat. 'It's not wrong,' he says. 'That's what's wrong. It's too right.'

choices.label: Wade out to examine the chapel structure
Read the procession pattern from the bank
Ask the ferryman what he hears before he sees them
Study the ground around the chapel door for the entry path

stages.text: The chapel is genuinely drowned — not half-submerged but fully under, accessible only through a flooded entrance that requires you to go through rather than around. The procession figures are bog zombies, still in the remnants of their burial clothes, still following a route they remember from when the chapel stood above water.

choices.label: Enter the chapel through the flooded entrance
Find an upper access point that is not submerged
Study the procession route to understand the route inside
Watch for what happens when the procession reaches the door

stages.text: A bog zombie lurches from the reeds at the chapel's side — not part of the procession but something that has been stationed here, drawn by the repeated gathering and now territorial about its proximity to it. It clutches a corroded hymn board to its chest with both hands and does not put it down.

choices.label: Fight the bog zombie
Speak the hymn from the corroded board
Pull the hymn board from its grip while it is distracted
Brace against it and force it back into the reeds

stages.text: Inside the chapel, the bell mechanism is still intact below the water — mounted on a stone frame, still swinging with each tide movement. The procession circles the altar in the exact pattern of a regular mass. To end this you need to either stop the bell or complete the rite it has been starting every morning for years.

choices.label: Reach the bell mechanism and physically stop the swing
Complete the mass sequence the procession is following
Find and remove the object anchoring the procession to the chapel
Sever the bell rope without disturbing the procession

stages.text: The bell falls silent. The procession stops mid-step and dissolves into the water without urgency, each figure simply ceasing to be present. The chapel is quiet for the first time in years, and the ferryman on the far bank stands up very straight and removes his hat.

choices.label: Search the now-still chapel for what the procession sought
Return to the ferryman and tell him what happened
Seal the chapel entrance so the cycle cannot restart

stages.text: The bell falls silent. In the chapel wreckage you recover a dark token once worn by assassins working the marsh roads — left there long ago, apparently, and the chapel kept tolling until someone came to take it.

quests.title: The Heron's Warning
quests.description: White herons keep abandoning the same watch post before nightfall as if fleeing something unseen.

stages.text: The watch post leans over black water and nobody has climbed it in a week. The herons that used it as a roost have been abandoning it each afternoon at the same time — a behavior that the marsh scouts have learned to take seriously. 'When the herons leave,' says the lead scout, 'something that frightens herons is coming. And herons are not small birds.'

choices.label: Climb the watch post to observe what the herons see
Study the post structure for signs of what has been disturbing it
Wait at the water's edge for the pattern to repeat
Ask the scouts when the abandonments started and what changed

stages.text: The watch post's platform shows marks of something very large gripping its lower supports from below — not climbing, but testing the structure's weight tolerance from the water. Whatever it is comes from the water side and reaches up. The scouting maps stored in the post's waterproof case are still there, which means the disturbance isn't about the post itself.

choices.label: Recover the scouting maps before the case is compromised
Study the grip marks for species and size
Watch the water below the post for movement
Set up a counterweight watch trigger on the lower supports

stages.text: A marsh ghoul crawls up the ladder from below — not the original attacker but something drawn by the same disturbance in the deeper water that has been driving everything else to the surface. It has a scouting map from a previous expedition wedged in its belt, which means it has been down there longer than this started.

choices.label: Fight the marsh ghoul
Kick it back into the water before it clears the railing
Leap to the roof and rain down from above
Retrieve the old map while it is still climbing

stages.text: The ghoul is dealt with, but from the top of the watch post you can see what has been disturbing the water — something large is circling the post from below, visible only as displacement and shadow. It has been circling longer than the ghouls have been rising. This is the cause, not the effect.

choices.label: Study the displacement pattern to identify what is below
Throw something heavy to draw it to the surface
Hold still until it surfaces to investigate the post directly
Use the watch post's signal torch to force it into visibility

stages.text: The creature surfaces briefly — a creature that shouldn't be in this part of the fen but has been driven here by something further in the marsh. It is not the problem; it is evidence of the problem. The maps recovered from the ghoul show where it came from, which is information worth considerably more than anyone expected.

choices.label: Deliver the recovered maps to the scout post immediately
Read the old maps for where this creature originated
Return to the scouts with a full account

stages.text: The maps are recovered and the surviving scouts gift you a weathered warden's cloak — the one their last warden was wearing when she went into the fen and came back with just the cloak and nothing to say about it.

quests.title: The Sunken Liturgy
quests.description: A ferryman heard chanting under the water and refuses to row near the old causeway anymore.

stages.text: At the causeway, each stone bears a drowned handprint and one unfinished prayer — the same words repeated stone after stone, always stopping at the same syllable, as though something interrupted the speaker at the same point every time.
