The ferryman will not approach within fifty yards. 'The water answers it,' he says. 'Every morning, something in the water sings back.'

choices.label: Read the prayers on the causeway stones for the repeated text
Listen beneath the water at the causeway edge
Map the handprint distribution for a pattern
Ask the ferryman what the answering sound resembles

stages.text: The prayer is a baptismal rite — the same one, repeated by dozens of people over what must have been years, all stopping at the moment of completion. The handprints are placement marks, not decoration. Someone was conducting mass baptisms on this causeway and something prevented every single one from finishing.

choices.label: Research the causeway's history to understand the interruption
Study what is in the water where the rite would have completed
Walk the causeway length without reading the stones
Speak the rite's completion aloud to see what responds

stages.text: A will-o'-wisp gathers into the shape of a drowned priest in the causeway mist — not aggressive but present, cycling through the interrupted moment of the rite on a loop. It has been here for years, repeating the same prayer, waiting for something to answer it properly. The water below moves in response to its cycle.

choices.label: Fight the will-o'-wisp to break the cycle
Complete the prayer correctly on the final stone
Provide the missing response the rite needs to complete
Speak the priest's name to acknowledge and release it

stages.text: The cycle breaks. The will-o'-wisp's form stabilises, holds for one breath, and then disperses into ordinary mist. The water below the causeway goes still — genuinely still, not the surface-tension stillness of deep movement, but settled.

choices.label: Read the water for what was keeping it disturbed
Speak a final sealing prayer over the stones
Check beneath the causeway stones for anything left behind
Return to the ferryman with the news

stages.text: The water is still. Left in a hollow stone near the final mark is a lacquered kit hidden there long ago by someone who came to retrieve something from the causeway and apparently found more than they expected. They left in a hurry and never came back.

choices.label: Retrieve the kit from the hollow stone
Ensure the causeway is marked as navigable again
Report to the ferryman that the causeway is safe

stages.text: The water stills. Left in a hollow stone is a lacquered killer's kit hidden there long ago — sealed, dry, complete, and apparently waiting for whoever finished what the causeway couldn't.

quests.title: Scales in the Mud
quests.description: Something enormous is shedding scales along the hunter paths and snapping boats in half at dusk.

stages.text: Broken reedboats line the bank in a way that looks less like accident and more like verdict. Deep parallel grooves in the mud end at the water line with nothing on the other side, and the hunters who reported this have not gone back since. 'The grooves go deep,' says the marsh warden. 'Whatever made them wasn't moving fast. It was deliberate.'

choices.label: Study the groove depth and spacing for creature type
Follow the bank looking for shed scales or other signs
Ask the hunters to describe what they heard before they saw
Wade the shallows to find where the creature rests

stages.text: The scales are real — palm-sized, iridescent in the bad light, and scattered along a quarter-mile of bank in a pattern that describes something very large moving through the shallows repeatedly. The creature has a regular route and has been using it for weeks. This is territory, not accident.

choices.label: Map the scale distribution to identify the territory boundaries
Find the creature's resting point within its territory
Set up at the narrowest part of its route
Check whether the creature has a den on the firmer ground

stages.text: You find a clutch of eggs in the reed bed — which changes the nature of the situation entirely. The creature is not being territorial arbitrarily; it is protecting a nest, and the hunters' paths run directly through its patrol zone. The swamp hydra surfaces forty yards away and orients toward you.

choices.label: Hold your position and let it assess you are not a threat
Back away from the eggs immediately and signal non-aggression
Study the hydra's approach to gauge its intent
Stand your ground to establish yourself as a boundary

stages.text: The swamp hydra rises in a storm of water and rot, smashing reeds with each thrashing head. It has decided the boundary conversation is over and is now expressing the position of a very large territorial animal with offspring. The hunters cannot use this route while it is active.

choices.label: Fight the swamp hydra
Lure it onto firmer ground where it is less effective
Destroy the nest to force a territorial retreat
Use the reed bed to obscure yourself and evade until it settles

stages.text: When the marsh calms, the hunters emerge from the bank where they've been watching. They debate what to do about the nest for approximately thirty seconds before the marsh warden says to leave it alone and find another route, which is the correct answer. They gift you the trophies recovered from the conflict.

choices.label: Accept the trophies the hunters offer for the risk
Help the hunters map an alternate route around the territory
Advise the warden on how to manage the territory boundary

stages.text: When the marsh calms, hunters craft a remarkable stormfeather quiver from recovered trophies and gift it to you — the kind of thing that takes a full day to make and requires materials you wouldn't want to collect yourself.

quests.title: The Fen Warden's Vows
quests.description: An old border warden's oathstones are being pulled from the bog one by one.

stages.text: Each stolen stone marks a promise once made to keep the marsh paths open to the desperate and lost. Seven stones placed by seven wardens over a hundred years, and now four of them are gone — pulled from the bog cleanly, no sign of struggle, no prints in the mud near where they stood. 'The paths are closing,' says the surviving warden. 'People will die in the marsh without markers.'

choices.label: Study the extraction sites for evidence of method
Read the oath inscriptions on the remaining stones
Track the removal pattern to predict the next target
Ask the warden who would benefit from the paths closing

stages.text: The extractions are not random — they follow a sequence designed to collapse the path network from the outside in, leaving the most-used central routes unguarded last. Whoever is doing this knows the marsh and knows the warden system. This is targeted sabotage by someone with detailed knowledge.

choices.label: Identify which stone will be taken next from the sequence
Set up at the predicted target stone before dark
Sense what entity is capable of this kind of deliberate action in the fen
Ask the witch tower's residents if they know anything

stages.text: A dark familiar swoops from the fog — sent to retrieve the fifth stone, acting with the precision of something following specific instructions from someone who values precision. It has not noticed you yet, but it will the moment you move from cover.

choices.label: Strike before it can reach the stone
Hide the oathstone and wait in ambush
Destroy the stone before the familiar can take it
Follow the familiar back to discover who sent it

stages.text: The dark familiar moves for the oathstone with focused certainty — it is not interested in you unless you force it to be. Its instructions are specific and it will complete them if you let it.

choices.label: Fight the dark familiar
Disrupt its concentration before it can grip the stone
Use the marsh terrain to prevent its escape with the stone
Drive it back with force before it can orient on you

stages.text: The familiar is dispersed and the oathstone stands. The warden places her hand on it in the silence after the fog and keeps it there for a long moment. 'The paths hold,' she says finally. Under the final stone there is a small cache — the wardens used to leave messages for each other here, sealed against the damp.

choices.label: Read the warden messages left in the cache
Seal the cache again for the next warden who needs it
Accept the surviving warden's offer of what the cache contains

stages.text: The vows endure. Beneath the final stone lies a gloomweave jerkin once worn by the first fen warden — dark, close-woven, and carrying fifty years of marsh work in its seams.

quests.title: Last Rite of Reeds
quests.description: A dying priest asks you to finish one final rite before the swamp swallows his chapel forever.

stages.text: He presses a key into your hand with both of his and does not let go immediately. 'Light the brazier. Read the names. Keep the marsh from keeping them.' The chapel is three hours into the fen — a route he can no longer walk. 'I have been trying to reach it for four years.' He releases the key. 'Please.'

choices.label: Ask the priest everything about the chapel's location
Ask about the rite itself and what it requires
Ask about the names you will need to read
Take the key and set out immediately

stages.text: The chapel is genuinely difficult to reach — the route the priest described is partially submerged and what isn't submerged has been overgrown in ways that make it hard to know where the path ends and the marsh begins. The chapel is visible from a distance: a stone spire that has somehow remained above water while everything around it has not.

choices.label: Navigate the submerged route by feel and reference points
Read the vegetation to find where the ground is firmest
Find the route the priest originally walked by its disruptions
Push through the overgrowth by force

stages.text: The chapel is intact inside — sealed against the marsh by something the priest presumably maintained for decades. The brazier is cold and the names are carved on every wall surface in a script that is still readable. An animated armor still guards the altar, swinging at any hand that reaches for the brazier, still doing the job it was wound up to do long ago.

choices.label: Fight the animated armor
Slip past it while it is in its patrol arc
Find the armor's deactivation point on the altar itself
Read the altar text to find the guardian's standing orders

stages.text: The armor is past. The brazier is within reach, cold and waiting. The names on the walls number in the hundreds — decades of marsh dead, carefully recorded, never read aloud. The key unlocks the brazier's fuel chamber, and the tinder inside is still dry. The priest thought of everything.

choices.label: Light the brazier and begin reading the names
Read all the names first before lighting anything
Light the brazier and let the firelight carry the names
Complete the rite in the exact sequence the priest described

stages.text: The names are spoken and the chapel can finally rest. The walls glow briefly as each name registers, and then go dark in a way that feels complete rather than empty. The marsh outside is quiet — not the suspicious quiet of something watching, but the quiet of something that has finally heard what it was waiting for.

choices.label: Seal the chapel properly before leaving
Return to the priest and tell him it is done
Take the crozier and carry the priest's work forward

stages.text: The names are spoken and the chapel can finally rest. The priest's crozier is yours to carry onward — old, well-made, and carried with purpose for longer than most things last.

quests.title: An Academy Errand
quests.description: A missing apprentice took dangerous star charts beyond the city walls and never came back.

stages.text: A dean of the academy sighs with the specific exhaustion of someone who has done this before. 'The fool wanted a clearer sky for her calculations. She took the restricted star charts — the ones we do not lend out — and went beyond the walls two nights ago without telling anyone.' She pushes an access pass across the desk. 'Bring her back. Or at least the charts.'

choices.label: Ask the dean what the restricted charts contain
Ask what the apprentice's area of study was
Ask the other apprentices what they know
Follow the mountain path the apprentice would have taken

stages.text: The mountain path leads toward a shattered observatory on the peak's shoulder — a research station abandoned after a drake attack three years ago. The apprentice's most recent notes suggest she planned to use the remaining dome to track a specific astronomical event. The notes end before she reached the dome.

choices.label: Read the notes carefully for the last recorded position
Track the apprentice's trail from where the notes stop
Assess the dome's structural safety from a distance
Move toward the dome quickly before the weather changes

stages.text: Near the shattered observatory, an ash drake circles over the abandoned charts that are spread across the broken dome roof. The apprentice is not here — her pack is, open and partially scattered, suggesting she ran. The drake is between you and the charts and is currently descending.

choices.label: Fight the ash drake
Snatch the charts between the drake's passes
Find the apprentice first — she is alive and nearby
Drive the drake off the dome with fire

stages.text: The charts are recovered but the apprentice is still missing. Her trail leads into the observatory's lower structure, which is less collapsed than the dome and apparently more interesting to hide in. Something has been following her tracks besides you — smaller than a drake, more deliberate.

choices.label: Follow her trail into the lower structure immediately
Identify what else has been following her
Call out for her to locate her by voice
Clear the lower structure before searching for her

stages.text: The apprentice is in the lower structure, alive and unharmed but completely unwilling to leave before she has finished her observation. She is also standing in the middle of three drake whelps who have nested there and are beginning to find her presence less amusing.

choices.label: Fight the drake whelps
Get the apprentice moving while the whelps are distracted
Make enough noise to drive the whelps toward the exit
Convince the apprentice that her data can wait

stages.text: The charts are saved. The academy rewards you with a talisman crafted for impossible long shots — the dean says it was the apprentice's graduation gift, which she forfeited by leaving without permission, and which you've clearly earned more honestly.

quests.title: The Shrine of Motes
quests.description: Ash has begun falling inside a sealed shrine where no fire should burn.

stages.text: The shrine keepers open the bronze doors only a finger's width. Warm ash spills through the crack — not the ash of incense or candle but of something larger, and it's been accumulating for days. 'We sealed it three nights ago,' says the senior keeper, 'and the ash keeps coming.' She pauses. 'There is also a sound. Low. Like chanting.'

choices.label: Examine the ash for composition and temperature
Listen at the doors for the chanting pattern
Ask the keepers what the shrine normally contains
Open the doors further to assess the interior

stages.text: Inside, the shrine is thick with drifting ash despite the sealed doors — which means the ash is being produced inside, not carried from outside. The shrine's central altar brazier is active and should not be. The keeper records show the last official lighting was six weeks ago. Someone has been here without authorization.

choices.label: Search for signs of unauthorised entry past the seal
Examine the brazier's fuel and fire type
Move through the ash without disturbing it
Locate where the chanting originates within the shrine

stages.text: A cinder cultist is in the shrine's inner sanctum, chanting over a guttering altar while ash swirls like snow around him. He has been here for days, feeding the shrine's fire with something that is not proper fuel, and the ritual he is performing has been building toward something that the ash is a symptom of.

choices.label: Interrupt the cultist physically
Study the ritual for what it is building toward
Smother the brazier before the cultist can complete the rite
Read the cultist's chant and counter it

stages.text: The cinder cultist has completed enough of the ritual that breaking it now requires dealing with him directly. He turns when he hears you — unsurprised, as though he expected this — and raises his staff with the calm of someone who has been interrupted before and knows it costs the interrupter more than it costs him.

choices.label: Fight the cinder cultist
Smother the brazier to cut his power before engaging
Drive him away from the altar to break his concentration
Throw ash in his eyes and strike while he's disoriented

stages.text: The shrine clears. The ash settles. The keepers enter cautiously and find the altar intact, the brazier cold, and the cultist's materials scattered across the sanctum floor in a way that tells a clear enough story for the city guard. They stand in the clear air for a long moment before anyone says anything.

choices.label: Help the keepers document what the cultist was attempting
Secure the cultist's materials for the city guard
Accept the keepers' formal thanks

stages.text: The shrine clears. In gratitude, the keepers robe you in cathedral vestments stitched against cinder and flame — made, the senior keeper says, for someone who would stand in fire to preserve what fire should not touch.

quests.title: Ballista on the Ridge
quests.description: A ridge ballista meant to defend Sunspire has gone silent after repeated drake attacks.

stages.text: From the city wall you can see the ballista jutting from the ridge like a broken tooth — still standing, technically, but clearly non-functional and uncrew since the last drake attack. The ridge garrison's sergeant is blunt: 'If another drake formation comes in from the eastern approach and that ballista isn't working, we lose the outer market in the first pass.'

choices.label: Study the ballista from the wall for visible damage
Ask the sergeant what happened during the last attack
Assess the route to the ridge for current hazards
Volunteer for the ridge immediately

stages.text: The route to the ridge is clear at the base but the exposed sections are very much not — drakes circle in pairs at the height the path crests, and the only way to reach the ballista position without being spotted is to move during the patrol gap, which is thirty seconds wide and requires covering sixty feet of exposed ground.

choices.label: Time the patrol gap and sprint the exposed section
Calculate the exact gap duration from the patrol arc
Find an alternate approach below the patrol altitude
Move during a weather obscuration

stages.text: The ballista is intact but the winch cable has been severed and the targeting arm is bent at a twenty-degree angle from impact. Repairing it is possible with the tools in the maintenance kit — which is still bolted to the frame — but it requires concentration, and a drake whelp has nested in the firing frame and considers your presence a direct challenge.

choices.label: Fight the drake whelp
Repair the winch while the whelp is distracted by movement below
Drive the whelp from the frame before the adults notice
Brace the targeting arm by hand to test range

stages.text: The whelp is clear but an adult ash drake has noticed the disturbance and is banking toward the ridge. The ballista is functional enough to use if the winch can be secured and the targeting arm can be manually held in position, but you have perhaps ninety seconds before the drake completes its approach.

choices.label: Get the ballista operational before the drake arrives
Brace the arm and hold it manually while it fires
Secure the winch cable in time to use the ballista
Read the drake's approach angle to maximize the firing window

stages.text: The ridge is secured. The garrison sends a crew up within the hour and the ballista is properly repaired by dusk. The sergeant watches the eastern approach with visible relief and presents you with the tool kit's resident moonshot bow — kept there as a secondary weapon, he says, and now officially surplus to requirements.

choices.label: Accept the bow and the garrison's respect
Help brief the new ballista crew on what you found
Scout the eastern approach before returning to the city

stages.text: The ridge is secured and the gunners present you with a moonshot bow in solemn thanks — designed for the ballista crew's secondary arms, built to reach ranges that most bows consider theoretical.

quests.title: Bastion of Embers
quests.description: An old flameguard bastion below the citadel has reignited its ward braziers and locked itself shut.

stages.text: The bastion glows from within as if preparing for a siege that ended centuries ago. The current garrison won't go near it. 'Three of my people tried,' says the garrison master. 'They came back shaken and one of them still won't say what she saw.' The doors are sealed — not locked, but sealed with the old thermal mechanism, the kind that only opens when the inside temperature drops or the right person opens it.

choices.label: Research the flameguard's original sealing mechanism
Assess the exterior for alternative entry points
Ask the garrison what their people saw inside
Force the thermal seal mechanism open from outside

stages.text: You get inside through a ventilation shaft the original builders didn't consider a security concern. The bastion is intact and active — the ward braziers burn in their original positions, the patrol routes are marked in scorched stone, and everything is exactly as it was when the order sealed it. Including the person who sealed it.

choices.label: Read the patrol routes to find the bastion's pattern
Move through the bastion without triggering its responses
Sense the nature of what is keeping this place active
Call out to whatever is maintaining the bastion

stages.text: The ember halls are wrong in a specific way — the heat is too even, the braziers too regular, and the patrol sounds too consistent. This is not a haunting. Something deliberate and functional is maintaining this place, and it is about to reach you on its patrol route.

choices.label: Hold your ground and assess what is coming
Find cover and study its patrol before engaging
Position yourself to control the engagement ground
Move toward it on your terms rather than waiting

stages.text: A flame knight marches the ember halls alone — the last officer of the original garrison, still executing the orders given when the bastion was sealed. It tests every intruder as though the city still depends on the outcome. It does not hate. It simply has a job and does it.

choices.label: Fight the flame knight
Speak the guard oath and challenge its purpose formally
Prove yourself by enduring the first exchange without retreating
Find the officer's standing orders and complete them

stages.text: The flame knight kneels. The bastion's braziers dim by half, as though the tension that maintained them has been resolved. The knight offers something from its pauldron — a seal from the original order, authorising access to the armoury behind the main hall.

choices.label: Accept the seal and access the armoury
Read the original orders that sealed this place
Report back to the garrison master with the full situation

stages.text: The knight kneels and surrenders the bastion's greatest shield: the Bulwark Aegis — carried into this place by the last garrison commander and kept here since, for whoever proved worthy of the trial.

quests.title: The Seventh Lens
quests.description: A shattered observatory lens is said to reveal weak points in dragonscale and human pride alike.

stages.text: The archivist spreads six lens cases on the table, each containing a flawless optical instrument. 'The seventh is missing. It was used to calibrate the observatory's targeting system — without it the calculations are theoretical.' She pauses. 'Cinder cultists took it two nights ago. They know what it does. They're going to use it to direct the dragon.'

choices.label: Study the six remaining lenses for the seventh's function
Ask the archivist where the cultists were last reported
Research the observatory's targeting system to understand the urgency
Move on the cultist position immediately before they use it

stages.text: The cultists were seen heading to the upper observatory — the one that was partially closed after the last drake attack but still has intact dome sections that give direct visual to Dragon Peak. A perfect position to direct something from above toward a specific target below.

choices.label: Approach the upper observatory without triggering the cultist alarm signals
Read the observatory's layout to plan the approach
Time the cultist movements from a distance before approaching
Force entry before they can complete the lens alignment

stages.text: At the summit dome, a cinder cultist guard stands at the only ground-level entry point, facing outward. Inside, you can hear the archivist's calibration equipment being misused. The lens is up there, being aligned. The guard is between you and it with about thirty seconds before the alignment is complete.

choices.label: Take down the guard quickly and move inside
Slip past the guard while their attention is on the horizon
Use reflected sunlight to blind the guard and enter
Create a distraction to draw the guard away from the door

stages.text: At the summit, a cinder cultist guards the lens beside a ring of ash and broken mirrors — part of the targeting assembly. He turns when you enter, evaluates the situation with professional speed, and decides the lens is worth defending with everything he has.

choices.label: Fight the cinder cultist
Shatter the mirror array to blind him with the reflected light
Knock the lens from its alignment before he can interfere
Break the cultist's concentration before he can activate the array

stages.text: The cultist falls and the lens is in your hands — intact, correctly ground, still warm from the cultist's handling. The targeting array is destroyed, the alignment aborted. Through the dome's aperture, Dragon Peak is visible against the clear sky — inactive, patient, not yet directed at anything specific.

choices.label: Deliver the lens to the archivist immediately
Document the cultist assembly for the garrison's intelligence
Destroy the targeting array completely before leaving

stages.text: The archivists restore the lens and award you a colossal greatblade forged to exploit the flaws it reveals — designed, the lead archivist says, to find the gap between what something appears to be and what it actually is.

quests.title: Pyre of the First Saint
quests.description: The oldest pyre in Sunspire has gone cold for the first time in living memory.

stages.text: Priests and soldiers kneel in uneasy silence around the cold pyre — the first cold this place has been since the founding, and the omens of the dragon only darken each day it remains unlit. 'Every soldier who has tried to enter the lower crypt has come back out,' says the high cleric. 'Not unwilling — unable. Something below rejects them.' She looks at you directly. 'We need someone who hasn't taken the soldier's oath.'

choices.label: Ask the high cleric everything about the pyre's history
Ask why the soldier's oath prevents entry
Ask what the crypt contains beyond the pyre mechanism
Request the crypt's layout before descending

stages.text: The crypt descends three levels and widens at the bottom into a chamber built around the pyre's roots — the original hearth that all the public flame above draws from. The air is cold in a way that has nothing to do with temperature. Something has been preventing the rekindling not through force but through presence.

choices.label: Read the crypt for what has changed recently
Sense the nature of the presence blocking the rekindling
Move carefully through the levels without disrupting whatever is here
Descend with purpose and make no concessions to the cold

stages.text: At the second level, a flame knight materialises from the chamber's ambient heat — not a guardian placed here but one that has been here for so long it has become part of the crypt's structure. It moves as though it is maintaining the cold, not fighting it. The presence that has been blocking the pyre is not hostile. It is waiting for something specific.

choices.label: Ask the flame knight what it is waiting for
Read the chamber for what the knight needs to see
Study the knight's behaviour pattern for the answer
Present yourself as the one who was sent to relight the pyre

stages.text: Below the second level, a second flame knight stands at a circle of failing wards between you and the sleeping coals of the original hearth. This one is not waiting — it is actively preventing the rekindling, maintaining the cold until whatever condition it was given has been met. It attacks with the grief of something that has been failing at its assigned purpose for decades.

choices.label: Fight the flame knight
Rekindle the wards before the knight can prevent it
Find the original condition and satisfy it without fighting
Push through the knight's defense and reach the hearth

stages.text: The knight falls. The hearth is before you — cold stone, old ash, and the last ember of the original fire, somehow still present after all this time, waiting at the very bottom of the ash in a way that defies everything. It responds to breath, which means it is not dead, merely conserved.

choices.label: Breathe life back into the ember directly
Feed the ember correctly with the proper fuel sequence
Cup the ember and carry it to the surface pyre
Strike new fire from the crypt's original flint and merge it with the ember

stages.text: The pyre roars back to life. The high clergy seal a halo ring around your wrist and name you a Protector of Sunspire — a title that has been given three times in the city's history, and carries with it the specific obligation to return if the pyre ever goes cold again.
